revision.text:
'''Shidori Shrine''' (倭文神社) refers to Shinto shrines bearing the name "Shidori" (also read as Shizuri, Shitori, or Shidori).
These shrines enshrine '''{{ill|Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|12=draft}}''' (also known as '''Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto''', '''Ameno Hachio''', or '''Takehatsuchi''') as the '''deity of weaving''', alongside '''{{ill|Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|ja|天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|12=draft}}''' (also known as '''Amano Yachichihime''' or '''Amanoorihime'''). These shrines were established by the '''{{ill|Shidori clan|ja|倭文氏|12=draft}}''', who worshipped Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto as their ancestral deity.
The '''origin of these shrines is attributed to''' the '''{{ill|Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine|ja|葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社|12=draft}}''' in '''Katsuragi, Nara Prefecture'''. However, while the introduction and promotion of silk weaving technology in Japan is credited to '''{{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇|12=draft}}''', some Shidori Shrines claim their origins date back to the reign of '''{{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|12=draft}}'''. Therefore, these shrines provide '''important historical evidence''' regarding '''when and where silk weaving developed in Japan (Wa).'''
Additionally, in the '''2016 animated film "[[:en:Your_Name|Your Name]]"''', the fictional '''{{ill|Miyamizu Shrine|ja|宮水神社|12=draft}}''' is depicted as enshrining '''Shidori deity Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto (倭文神建葉槌命)'''
== Shizuri Shrines of the Engishiki ==
*'''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Isesaki)|ja|倭文神社 (伊勢崎市)|12=draft}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Shidori-go, Nanba District, Kōzuke Province''' – (Higashi-Kaminomiya-cho, {{ill|Isesaki, Gunma|ja|伊勢崎市|12=draft}}, [[Gunma Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shizu Shrine|ja|静神社|12=draft}} (静神社)''' in '''Kuji District, Hitachi Province''' – ({{ill|Naka, Ibaraki|ja|那珂市|12=draft}}, [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Nirasaki)|ja|倭文神社 (韮崎市)|12=draft}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Koma District, Kai Province''' – (Miyakubo, {{ill|Nirasaki, Yamanashi|ja|韮崎市|12=draft}}, [[Yamanashi Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Fujinomiya)|ja|倭文神社 (富士宮市)|12=draft}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Fuji District, Suruga Province''' – (Hoshiyama, {{ill|Fujinomiya, Shizuoka|ja|富士宮市|12=draft}}, [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]) * '''Shidori Shrine (倭文神社)''' in '''Tagata District, Izu Province''' – Identified with multiple candidate shrines, including '''{{ill|Kuwado Shrine|ja|鍬戸神社|12=draft}}''' ({{ill|Mishima, Shizuoka|ja|三島市|12=draft}}, [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]) * '''Shidori Shrine (倭文神社)''' in '''Suzuka District, Ise Province''' – '''{{ill|Kasado Shrine|ja|加佐登神社|12=draft}}''' (merged into [[Suzuka, Mie|Suzuka]], [[Mie Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Maizuru)|ja|倭文神社 (舞鶴市)|12=draft}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Kasa District, Tango Province''' – (Imada, {{ill|Maizuru, Kyoto|ja|舞鶴市|12=draft}}, [[Kyoto Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Yosano)|ja|倭文神社 (与謝野町)|12=draft}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Yosa District, Tango Province''' – (Mikawachi, {{ill|Yosano, Kyoto|ja|与謝野町|12=draft}}, [[Kyoto Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Asago)|ja|倭文神社 (朝来市)|12=draft}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Asago District, Tajima Province''' – (Maruyama, {{ill|Asago, Hyogo|ja|朝来市|12=draft}}, [[Hyogo Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Tottori)|ja|倭文神社 (鳥取市)|12=draft}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Takakusa District, Inaba Province''' – (Shidori, {{ill|Tottori, Tottori|ja|鳥取市|12=draft}}, [[Tottori Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|ja|倭文神社 (湯梨浜町)|1=Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|12=draft}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Kawamura District, Hōki Province''' – (Miyauchi, {{ill|Yurihama, Tottori|ja|湯梨浜町|12=draft}}, [[Tottori Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Kurayoshi)|ja|倭文神社 (倉吉市)|12=draft}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Kume District, Hōki Province''' – (Shizu, {{ill|Kurayoshi, Tottori|ja|倉吉市|12=draft}}, [[Tottori Prefecture]])
== Other shrines == *'''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Tono)|ja|倭文神社 (遠野市)|12=draft}}''' (Tsuchibuchi-cho, {{ill|Tono, Iwate|ja|遠野市|12=draft}}, [[Iwate Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Nara)|ja|倭文神社 (奈良市)|12=draft}}''' (Nishikujo-cho, {{ill|Nara, Nara|ja|奈良市|12=draft}}, [[Nara Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Tsuyama)|ja|倭文神社 (津山市油木北)|12=draft}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Shidori-go, Kume District, Mimasaka Province''' – (Yukikita, {{ill|Tsuyama, Okayama|ja|津山市|12=draft}}, [[Okayama Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Awaji)|ja|倭文神社 (あわじ市倭文委文)|12=draft}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Shidori-go, Mihara District, Awaji Province''' – (Shidori, {{ill|Minamiawaji, Hyogo|ja|南あわじ市|12=draft}}, [[Hyogo Prefecture]])
Additionally, '''{{ill|Ōmika Shrine|8=ja|9=大甕神社|ja|大甕神社|12=draft}}''' ({{ill|Hitachi, Ibaraki|ja|日立市|12=draft}}, [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]) also enshrines '''Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto'''. Similarly, '''{{ill|Ishii Shrine|ja|石井神社|12=draft}}''' ({{ill|Kasama, Ibaraki|ja|笠間市|12=draft}}, [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]) enshrines the same deity, with a large sacred rock called "Mitarai" located to the south. == See Also ==
* '''{{ill|Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|12=draft}}''' (also known as Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto) * '''{{ill|Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|ja|天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|12=draft}}''' (also known as Amanoorihime)

== Other shrines == *'''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Tono)|ja|倭文神社 (遠野市)|12=draft}}''' (Tsuchibuchi-cho, {{ill|Tono, Iwate|ja|遠野市|12=draft}}, [[Iwate Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Nara)|ja|倭文神社 (奈良市)|12=draft}}''' (Nishikujo-cho, {{ill|Nara, Nara|ja|奈良市|12=draft}}, [[Nara Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Tsuyama)|ja|倭文神社 (津山市油木北)|12=draft}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Shidori-go, Kume District, Mimasaka Province''' – (Yukikita, {{ill|Tsuyama, Okayama|ja|津山市|12=draft}}, [[Okayama Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Awaji)|ja|倭文神社 (あわじ市倭文委文)|12=draft}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Shidori-go, Mihara District, Awaji Province''' – (Shidori, {{ill|Minamiawaji, Hyogo|ja|南あわじ市|12=draft}}, [[Hyogo Prefecture]])
Additionally, '''{{ill|Ōmika Shrine|8=ja|9=大甕神社|ja|大甕神社|12=draft}}''' ({{ill|Hitachi, Ibaraki|ja|日立市|12=draft}}, [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]) also enshrines '''Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto'''. Similarly, '''{{ill|Ishii Shrine|ja|石井神社|12=draft}}''' ({{ill|Kasama, Ibaraki|ja|笠間市|12=draft}}, [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]) enshrines the same deity, with a large sacred rock called "Mitarai" located to the south. == See Also ==
* '''{{ill|Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|12=draft}}''' (also known as Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto) * '''{{ill|Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|ja|天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|12=draft}}''' (also known as Amanoorihime)

revision.comment: Text replacement - "|倭文氏|" to "|倭文部|1=Shidori clan|2=ja|3=倭文氏|4=zh|5=倭文氏|6=de|7=Shizuri Clan|8=fr|9=Clan Shizuri|lt=Shizuri Bemin (vassals of the Shizuri clan)|lt=Shidori clan|"
revision.text:
'''Shidori Shrine''' (倭文神社) refers to Shinto shrines bearing the name "Shidori" (also read as Shizuri, Shitori, or Shidori).
These shrines enshrine '''{{ill|Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|12=draft}}''' (also known as '''Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto''', '''Ameno Hachio''', or '''Takehatsuchi''') as the '''deity of weaving''', alongside '''{{ill|Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|ja|天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|12=draft}}''' (also known as '''Amano Yachichihime''' or '''Amanoorihime'''). These shrines were established by the '''{{ill|Shidori clan|ja|倭文部|1=Shidori clan|2=ja|3=倭文氏|4=zh|5=倭文氏|6=de|7=Shizuri Clan|8=fr|9=Clan Shizuri|lt=Shizuri Bemin (vassals of the Shizuri clan)|lt=Shidori clan|12=draft}}''', who worshipped Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto as their ancestral deity.
The '''origin of these shrines is attributed to''' the '''{{ill|Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine|ja|葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社|12=draft}}''' in '''Katsuragi, Nara Prefecture'''. However, while the introduction and promotion of silk weaving technology in Japan is credited to '''{{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇|12=draft}}''', some Shidori Shrines claim their origins date back to the reign of '''{{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|12=draft}}'''. Therefore, these shrines provide '''important historical evidence''' regarding '''when and where silk weaving developed in Japan (Wa).'''
Additionally, in the '''2016 animated film "[[:en:Your_Name|Your Name]]"''', the fictional '''{{ill|Miyamizu Shrine|ja|宮水神社|12=draft}}''' is depicted as enshrining '''Shidori deity Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto (倭文神建葉槌命)'''
== Shizuri Shrines of the Engishiki ==
*'''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Isesaki)|ja|倭文神社 (伊勢崎市)|12=draft}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Shidori-go, Nanba District, Kōzuke Province''' – (Higashi-Kaminomiya-cho, {{ill|Isesaki, Gunma|ja|伊勢崎市|12=draft}}, [[Gunma Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shizu Shrine|ja|静神社|12=draft}} (静神社)''' in '''Kuji District, Hitachi Province''' – ({{ill|Naka, Ibaraki|ja|那珂市|12=draft}}, [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Nirasaki)|ja|倭文神社 (韮崎市)|12=draft}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Koma District, Kai Province''' – (Miyakubo, {{ill|Nirasaki, Yamanashi|ja|韮崎市|12=draft}}, [[Yamanashi Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Fujinomiya)|ja|倭文神社 (富士宮市)|12=draft}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Fuji District, Suruga Province''' – (Hoshiyama, {{ill|Fujinomiya, Shizuoka|ja|富士宮市|12=draft}}, [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]) * '''Shidori Shrine (倭文神社)''' in '''Tagata District, Izu Province''' – Identified with multiple candidate shrines, including '''{{ill|Kuwado Shrine|ja|鍬戸神社|12=draft}}''' ({{ill|Mishima, Shizuoka|ja|三島市|12=draft}}, [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]) * '''Shidori Shrine (倭文神社)''' in '''Suzuka District, Ise Province''' – '''{{ill|Kasado Shrine|ja|加佐登神社|12=draft}}''' (merged into [[Suzuka, Mie|Suzuka]], [[Mie Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Maizuru)|ja|倭文神社 (舞鶴市)|12=draft}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Kasa District, Tango Province''' – (Imada, {{ill|Maizuru, Kyoto|ja|舞鶴市|12=draft}}, [[Kyoto Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Yosano)|ja|倭文神社 (与謝野町)|12=draft}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Yosa District, Tango Province''' – (Mikawachi, {{ill|Yosano, Kyoto|ja|与謝野町|12=draft}}, [[Kyoto Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Asago)|ja|倭文神社 (朝来市)|12=draft}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Asago District, Tajima Province''' – (Maruyama, {{ill|Asago, Hyogo|ja|朝来市|12=draft}}, [[Hyogo Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Tottori)|ja|倭文神社 (鳥取市)|12=draft}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Takakusa District, Inaba Province''' – (Shidori, {{ill|Tottori, Tottori|ja|鳥取市|12=draft}}, [[Tottori Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|ja|倭文神社 (湯梨浜町)|1=Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|12=draft}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Kawamura District, Hōki Province''' – (Miyauchi, {{ill|Yurihama, Tottori|ja|湯梨浜町|12=draft}}, [[Tottori Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Kurayoshi)|ja|倭文神社 (倉吉市)|12=draft}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Kume District, Hōki Province''' – (Shizu, {{ill|Kurayoshi, Tottori|ja|倉吉市|12=draft}}, [[Tottori Prefecture]])
== Other shrines == *'''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Tono)|ja|倭文神社 (遠野市)|12=draft}}''' (Tsuchibuchi-cho, {{ill|Tono, Iwate|ja|遠野市|12=draft}}, [[Iwate Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Nara)|ja|倭文神社 (奈良市)|12=draft}}''' (Nishikujo-cho, {{ill|Nara, Nara|ja|奈良市|12=draft}}, [[Nara Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Tsuyama)|ja|倭文神社 (津山市油木北)|12=draft}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Shidori-go, Kume District, Mimasaka Province''' – (Yukikita, {{ill|Tsuyama, Okayama|ja|津山市|12=draft}}, [[Okayama Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Awaji)|ja|倭文神社 (あわじ市倭文委文)|12=draft}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Shidori-go, Mihara District, Awaji Province''' – (Shidori, {{ill|Minamiawaji, Hyogo|ja|南あわじ市|12=draft}}, [[Hyogo Prefecture]])
Additionally, '''{{ill|Ōmika Shrine|8=ja|9=大甕神社|ja|大甕神社|12=draft}}''' ({{ill|Hitachi, Ibaraki|ja|日立市|12=draft}}, [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]) also enshrines '''Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto'''. Similarly, '''{{ill|Ishii Shrine|ja|石井神社|12=draft}}''' ({{ill|Kasama, Ibaraki|ja|笠間市|12=draft}}, [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]) enshrines the same deity, with a large sacred rock called "Mitarai" located to the south. == See Also ==
* '''{{ill|Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|12=draft}}''' (also known as Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto) * '''{{ill|Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|ja|天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|12=draft}}''' (also known as Amanoorihime)

== Other shrines == *'''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Tono)|ja|倭文神社 (遠野市)|12=draft}}''' (Tsuchibuchi-cho, {{ill|Tono, Iwate|ja|遠野市|12=draft}}, [[Iwate Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Nara)|ja|倭文神社 (奈良市)|12=draft}}''' (Nishikujo-cho, {{ill|Nara, Nara|ja|奈良市|12=draft}}, [[Nara Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Tsuyama)|ja|倭文神社 (津山市油木北)|12=draft}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Shidori-go, Kume District, Mimasaka Province''' – (Yukikita, {{ill|Tsuyama, Okayama|ja|津山市|12=draft}}, [[Okayama Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Awaji)|ja|倭文神社 (あわじ市倭文委文)|12=draft}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Shidori-go, Mihara District, Awaji Province''' – (Shidori, {{ill|Minamiawaji, Hyogo|ja|南あわじ市|12=draft}}, [[Hyogo Prefecture]])
Additionally, '''{{ill|Ōmika Shrine|8=ja|9=大甕神社|ja|大甕神社|12=draft}}''' ({{ill|Hitachi, Ibaraki|ja|日立市|12=draft}}, [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]) also enshrines '''Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto'''. Similarly, '''{{ill|Ishii Shrine|ja|石井神社|12=draft}}''' ({{ill|Kasama, Ibaraki|ja|笠間市|12=draft}}, [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]) enshrines the same deity, with a large sacred rock called "Mitarai" located to the south. == See Also ==
* '''{{ill|Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|12=draft}}''' (also known as Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto) * '''{{ill|Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|ja|天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|12=draft}}''' (also known as Amanoorihime)

revision.text:
'''倭文神社'''（しとり、しずり、しどり、しとおり）は、｢倭文」を社名に持つ[[神社]]。
いずれも機織の神である[[天羽槌雄神|建葉槌命]]（タケハツチ。天羽雷命・天羽槌雄・武羽槌雄などとも）と[[天棚機姫神|棚機姫命]]（たなばたひめ。天之八千千比売・天衣織女などとも）を祀る神社で、建葉槌命を祖神とする[[倭文氏]]によって祀られたものである。その本源は、[[葛木倭文坐天羽雷命神社]]（奈良県葛城市）とされている。しかし、絹織物の技術は[[仁徳天皇]]により導入振興されたとされるも、[[崇神天皇]]期にその創始を唱える倭文神社もあり、日本（倭）においていつ頃どこで絹織物が発達したかを考えるうえで、この神社のある場所や由緒は貴重な資料といえる。
また、2016年公開の映画「君の名は｡」の中に出てくる[[宮水神社]]も倭文神建葉槌命を祀っている設定である。
== 主な倭文神社 == [[延喜式神名帳]]には、以下の社名が見える。
*上野国那波郡倭文郷 [[倭文神社 (伊勢崎市)|倭文神社]] （[[群馬県]][[伊勢崎市]]東上之宮町） *常陸国久慈郡 [[静神社]] （[[茨城県]][[那珂市]]静） *甲斐国巨摩郡 [[倭文神社 (韮崎市)|倭文神社]] （[[山梨県]][[韮崎市]]穂坂町宮久保） *駿河国富士郡 [[倭文神社 (富士宮市)|倭文神社]] （[[静岡県]][[富士宮市]]星山） *伊豆国田方郡 倭文神社 – [[鍬戸神社]]（静岡県[[三島市]]）ほか論社複数 *伊勢国鈴鹿郡 倭文神社 – [[加佐登神社]]（[[三重県]][[鈴鹿市]]）に合祀 *丹後国加佐郡 [[倭文神社 (舞鶴市)|倭文神社]] （[[京都府]][[舞鶴市]]今田） *丹後国与謝郡 [[倭文神社 (与謝野町)|倭文神社]] （京都府[[与謝郡]][[与謝野町]]三河内） *但馬郡朝来郡 [[倭文神社 (朝来市)|倭文神社]] （[[兵庫県]][[朝来市]]生野町円山） *因幡国高草郡 [[倭文神社 (鳥取市)|倭文神社]] （[[鳥取県]][[鳥取市]]倭文） *伯耆国河村郡 [[倭文神社 (湯梨浜町)|倭文神社]] （鳥取県[[東伯郡]][[湯梨浜町]]宮内） *伯耆国久米郡 [[倭文神社 (倉吉市)|倭文神社]] （鳥取県[[倉吉市]]志津）
他に、以下の倭文神社も著名である。 *[[倭文神社 (遠野市)|倭文神社]] （[[岩手県]][[遠野市]]土淵町） *[[倭文神社 (奈良市)|倭文神社]] （[[奈良県]][[奈良市]]西九条町） *美作国久米郡倭文郷 [[倭文神社 (津山市油木北)|倭文神社]] （[[岡山県]][[津山市]]油木北） *淡路国三原郡倭文郷 [[倭文神社 (あわじ市倭文委文)|倭文神社]] （[[兵庫県]][[南あわじ市]]倭文委文）
また、[[大甕神社]]（茨城県[[日立市]]）にも武葉槌命が祀られている。同じく、[[石井神社]]（茨城県[[笠間市]]）にも武葉槌命が祀られ、南方には御手洗という巨石が存在する。
== 関連項目 ==
* 建葉槌命（[[天羽槌雄神]]） * 棚機姫命（[[天棚機姫神]]）
[[Category:神社 (祭神・信仰別)|しすりしんしや]]

いずれも機織の神である[[天羽槌雄神|建葉槌命]]（タケハツチ。天羽雷命・天羽槌雄・武羽槌雄などとも）と[[天棚機姫神|棚機姫命]]（たなばたひめ。天之八千千比売・天衣織女などとも）を祀る神社で、建葉槌命を祖神とする[[倭文氏]]によって祀られたものである。その本源は、[[葛木倭文坐天羽雷命神社]]（奈良県葛城市）とされている。しかし、絹織物の技術は[[仁徳天皇]]により導入振興されたとされるも、[[崇神天皇]]期にその創始を唱える倭文神社もあり、日本（倭）においていつ頃どこで絹織物が発達したかを考えるうえで、この神社のある場所や由緒は貴重な資料といえる。

他に、以下の倭文神社も著名である。 *[[倭文神社 (遠野市)|倭文神社]] （[[岩手県]][[遠野市]]土淵町） *[[倭文神社 (奈良市)|倭文神社]] （[[奈良県]][[奈良市]]西九条町） *美作国久米郡倭文郷 [[倭文神社 (津山市油木北)|倭文神社]] （[[岡山県]][[津山市]]油木北） *淡路国三原郡倭文郷 [[倭文神社 (あわじ市倭文委文)|倭文神社]] （[[兵庫県]][[南あわじ市]]倭文委文）
また、[[大甕神社]]（茨城県[[日立市]]）にも武葉槌命が祀られている。同じく、[[石井神社]]（茨城県[[笠間市]]）にも武葉槌命が祀られ、南方には御手洗という巨石が存在する。

revision.text:
'''Shidori Shrine''' (倭文神社) refers to Shinto shrines bearing the name "Shidori" (also read as Shizuri, Shitori, or Shidori).
These shrines enshrine '''{{ill|Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' (also known as '''Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto''', '''Ameno Hachio''', or '''Takehatsuchi''') as the '''deity of weaving''', alongside '''{{ill|Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|ja|天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' (also known as '''Amano Yachichihime''' or '''Amanoorihime'''). These shrines were established by the '''{{ill|Shizuri clan|ja|倭文部|1=Shizuri clan|2=ja|3=倭文氏|4=zh|5=倭文氏|6=de|7=Shizuri Clan|8=fr|9=Clan Shizuri|lt=Shizuri Bemin (vassals of the Shizuri clan)|lt=Shizuri clan|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''', who worshipped Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto as their ancestral deity.
The '''origin of these shrines is attributed to''' the '''{{ill|Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine|ja|葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' in '''Katsuragi, Nara Prefecture'''. However, while the introduction and promotion of silk weaving technology in Japan is credited to '''{{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''', some Shidori Shrines claim their origins date back to the reign of '''{{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'''. Therefore, these shrines provide '''important historical evidence''' regarding '''when and where silk weaving developed in Japan (Wa).'''
Additionally, in the '''2016 animated film "[[:en:Your_Name|Your Name]]"''', the fictional '''{{ill|Miyamizu Shrine|ja|宮水神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' is depicted as enshrining '''Shidori deity Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto (倭文神建葉槌命)'''
== Shizuri Shrines of the Engishiki ==
*'''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Isesaki)|ja|倭文神社 (伊勢崎市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Shidori-go, Nanba District, Kōzuke Province''' – (Higashi-Kaminomiya-cho, {{ill|Isesaki, Gunma|ja|伊勢崎市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, [[Gunma Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shizu Shrine|ja|静神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (静神社)''' in '''Kuji District, Hitachi Province''' – ({{ill|Naka, Ibaraki|ja|那珂市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Nirasaki)|ja|倭文神社 (韮崎市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Koma District, Kai Province''' – (Miyakubo, {{ill|Nirasaki, Yamanashi|ja|韮崎市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, [[Yamanashi Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Fujinomiya)|ja|倭文神社 (富士宮市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Fuji District, Suruga Province''' – (Hoshiyama, {{ill|Fujinomiya, Shizuoka|ja|富士宮市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]) * '''Shidori Shrine (倭文神社)''' in '''Tagata District, Izu Province''' – Identified with multiple candidate shrines, including '''{{ill|Kuwado Shrine|ja|鍬戸神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' ({{ill|Mishima, Shizuoka|ja|三島市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]) * '''Shidori Shrine (倭文神社)''' in '''Suzuka District, Ise Province''' – '''{{ill|Kasado Shrine|ja|加佐登神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' (merged into [[Suzuka, Mie|Suzuka]], [[Mie Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Maizuru)|ja|倭文神社 (舞鶴市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Kasa District, Tango Province''' – (Imada, {{ill|Maizuru, Kyoto|ja|舞鶴市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, [[Kyoto Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Yosano)|ja|倭文神社 (与謝野町)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Yosa District, Tango Province''' – (Mikawachi, {{ill|Yosano, Kyoto|ja|与謝野町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, [[Kyoto Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Asago)|ja|倭文神社 (朝来市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Asago District, Tajima Province''' – (Maruyama, {{ill|Asago, Hyogo|ja|朝来市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, [[Hyogo Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Tottori)|ja|倭文神社 (鳥取市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Takakusa District, Inaba Province''' – (Shidori, {{ill|Tottori, Tottori|ja|鳥取市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, [[Tottori Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|ja|倭文神社 (湯梨浜町)|1=Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Kawamura District, Hōki Province''' – (Miyauchi, {{ill|Yurihama, Tottori|ja|湯梨浜町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, [[Tottori Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Kurayoshi)|ja|倭文神社 (倉吉市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Kume District, Hōki Province''' – (Shizu, {{ill|Kurayoshi, Tottori|ja|倉吉市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, [[Tottori Prefecture]])
== Other shrines == *'''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Tono)|ja|倭文神社 (遠野市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' (Tsuchibuchi-cho, {{ill|Tono, Iwate|ja|遠野市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, [[Iwate Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Nara)|ja|倭文神社 (奈良市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' (Nishikujo-cho, {{ill|Nara, Nara|ja|奈良市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, [[Nara Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Tsuyama)|ja|倭文神社 (津山市油木北)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Shidori-go, Kume District, Mimasaka Province''' – (Yukikita, {{ill|Tsuyama, Okayama|ja|津山市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, [[Okayama Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Awaji)|ja|倭文神社 (あわじ市倭文委文)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Shidori-go, Mihara District, Awaji Province''' – (Shidori, {{ill|Minamiawaji, Hyogo|ja|南あわじ市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, [[Hyogo Prefecture]])
Additionally, '''{{ill|Ōmika Shrine|8=ja|9=大甕神社|ja|大甕神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' ({{ill|Hitachi, Ibaraki|ja|日立市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]) also enshrines '''Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto'''. Similarly, '''{{ill|Ishii Shrine|ja|石井神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' ({{ill|Kasama, Ibaraki|ja|笠間市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]) enshrines the same deity, with a large sacred rock called "Mitarai" located to the south. == See Also ==

revision.text:
'''Shidori Shrine''' (倭文神社) refers to Shinto shrines bearing the name "Shidori" (also read as Shizuri, Shitori, or Shidori).
These shrines enshrine '''{{ill|Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' (also known as '''Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto''', '''Ameno Hachio''', or '''Takehatsuchi''') as the '''deity of weaving''', alongside '''{{ill|Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|ja|天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' (also known as '''Amano Yachichihime''' or '''Amanoorihime'''). These shrines were established by the '''{{ill|Shizuri clan|ja|倭文部|1=Shizuri clan|2=ja|3=倭文氏|4=zh|5=倭文氏|6=de|7=Shizuri Clan|8=fr|9=Clan Shizuri|lt=Shizuri Bemin (vassals of the Shizuri clan)|lt=Shizuri clan|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''', who worshipped Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto as their ancestral deity.
The '''origin of these shrines is attributed to''' the '''{{ill|Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine|ja|葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' in '''Katsuragi, Nara Prefecture'''. However, while the introduction and promotion of silk weaving technology in Japan is credited to '''{{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''', some Shidori Shrines claim their origins date back to the reign of '''{{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'''. Therefore, these shrines provide '''important historical evidence''' regarding '''when and where silk weaving developed in Japan (Wa).'''
Additionally, in the '''2016 animated film "[[:en:Your_Name|Your Name]]"''', the fictional '''{{ill|Miyamizu Shrine|ja|宮水神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' is depicted as enshrining '''Shidori deity Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto (倭文神建葉槌命)'''
== Shizuri Shrines of the Engishiki ==
*'''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Isesaki)|ja|倭文神社 (伊勢崎市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Shidori-go, Nanba District, Kōzuke Province''' – (Higashi-Kaminomiya-cho, {{ill|Isesaki, Gunma|ja|伊勢崎市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, [[Gunma Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shizu Shrine|ja|静神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (静神社)''' in '''Kuji District, Hitachi Province''' – ({{ill|Naka, Ibaraki|ja|那珂市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Nirasaki)|ja|倭文神社 (韮崎市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Koma District, Kai Province''' – (Miyakubo, {{ill|Nirasaki, Yamanashi|ja|韮崎市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, [[Yamanashi Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Fujinomiya)|ja|倭文神社 (富士宮市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Fuji District, Suruga Province''' – (Hoshiyama, {{ill|Fujinomiya, Shizuoka|ja|富士宮市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]) * '''Shidori Shrine (倭文神社)''' in '''Tagata District, Izu Province''' – Identified with multiple candidate shrines, including '''{{ill|Kuwado Shrine|ja|鍬戸神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' ({{ill|Mishima, Shizuoka|ja|三島市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]) * '''Shidori Shrine (倭文神社)''' in '''Suzuka District, Ise Province''' – '''{{ill|Kasado Shrine|ja|加佐登神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' (merged into [[Suzuka, Mie|Suzuka]], [[Mie Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Maizuru)|ja|倭文神社 (舞鶴市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Kasa District, Tango Province''' – (Imada, {{ill|Maizuru, Kyoto|ja|舞鶴市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, [[Kyoto Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Yosano)|ja|倭文神社 (与謝野町)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Yosa District, Tango Province''' – (Mikawachi, {{ill|Yosano, Kyoto|ja|与謝野町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, [[Kyoto Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Asago)|ja|倭文神社 (朝来市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Asago District, Tajima Province''' – (Maruyama, {{ill|Asago, Hyogo|ja|朝来市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, [[Hyogo Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Tottori)|ja|倭文神社 (鳥取市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Takakusa District, Inaba Province''' – (Shidori, {{ill|Tottori, Tottori|ja|鳥取市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, [[Tottori Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|ja|倭文神社 (湯梨浜町)|1=Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Kawamura District, Hōki Province''' – (Miyauchi, {{ill|Yurihama, Tottori|ja|湯梨浜町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, [[Tottori Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Kurayoshi)|ja|倭文神社 (倉吉市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Kume District, Hōki Province''' – (Shizu, {{ill|Kurayoshi, Tottori|ja|倉吉市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, [[Tottori Prefecture]])
== Other shrines == *'''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Tono)|ja|倭文神社 (遠野市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' (Tsuchibuchi-cho, {{ill|Tono, Iwate|ja|遠野市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, [[Iwate Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Nara)|ja|倭文神社 (奈良市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' (Nishikujo-cho, {{ill|Nara, Nara|ja|奈良市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, [[Nara Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Tsuyama)|ja|倭文神社 (津山市油木北)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Shidori-go, Kume District, Mimasaka Province''' – (Yukikita, {{ill|Tsuyama, Okayama|ja|津山市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, [[Okayama Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Awaji)|ja|倭文神社 (あわじ市倭文委文)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Shidori-go, Mihara District, Awaji Province''' – (Shidori, {{ill|Minamiawaji, Hyogo|ja|南あわじ市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, [[Hyogo Prefecture]])
Additionally, '''{{ill|Ōmika Shrine|8=ja|9=大甕神社|ja|大甕神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' ({{ill|Hitachi, Ibaraki|ja|日立市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]) also enshrines '''Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto'''. Similarly, '''{{ill|Ishii Shrine|ja|石井神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' ({{ill|Kasama, Ibaraki|ja|笠間市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]) enshrines the same deity, with a large sacred rock called "Mitarai" located to the south. == See Also ==
* '''{{ill|Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' (also known as Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto) * '''{{ill|Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|ja|天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' (also known as Amanoorihime)

(Tottori)|ja|倭文神社 (鳥取市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Takakusa District, Inaba Province''' – (Shidori, {{ill|Tottori, Tottori|ja|鳥取市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, [[Tottori Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|ja|倭文神社 (湯梨浜町)|1=Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Kawamura District, Hōki Province''' – (Miyauchi, {{ill|Yurihama, Tottori|ja|湯梨浜町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, [[Tottori Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Kurayoshi)|ja|倭文神社 (倉吉市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Kume District, Hōki Province''' – (Shizu, {{ill|Kurayoshi, Tottori|ja|倉吉市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, [[Tottori Prefecture]])
== Other shrines == *'''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Tono)|ja|倭文神社 (遠野市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' (Tsuchibuchi-cho, {{ill|Tono, Iwate|ja|遠野市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, [[Iwate Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Nara)|ja|倭文神社 (奈良市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' (Nishikujo-cho, {{ill|Nara, Nara|ja|奈良市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, [[Nara Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Tsuyama)|ja|倭文神社 (津山市油木北)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Shidori-go, Kume District, Mimasaka Province''' – (Yukikita, {{ill|Tsuyama, Okayama|ja|津山市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, [[Okayama Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Awaji)|ja|倭文神社 (あわじ市倭文委文)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Shidori-go, Mihara District, Awaji Province''' – (Shidori, {{ill|Minamiawaji, Hyogo|ja|南あわじ市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, [[Hyōgo Prefecture]])
Additionally, '''{{ill|Ōmika Shrine|8=ja|9=大甕神社|ja|大甕神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' ({{ill|Hitachi, Ibaraki|ja|日立市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]) also enshrines '''Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto'''. Similarly, '''{{ill|Ishii Shrine|ja|石井神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' ({{ill|Kasama, Ibaraki|ja|笠間市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]) enshrines the same deity, with a large sacred rock called "Mitarai" located to the south. == See Also ==
* '''{{ill|Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' (also known as Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto) * '''{{ill|Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|ja|天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' (also known as Amanoorihime)

revision.comment: Bot: add simplewiki draft link to ill templates
revision.text:
'''Shidori Shrine''' (倭文神社) refers to Shinto shrines bearing the name "Shidori" (also read as Shizuri, Shitori, or Shidori).
These shrines enshrine '''{{ill|Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}}''' (also known as '''Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto''', '''Ameno Hachio''', or '''Takehatsuchi''') as the '''deity of weaving''', alongside '''{{ill|Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|ja|天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}''' (also known as '''Amano Yachichihime''' or '''Amanoorihime'''). These shrines were established by the '''{{ill|Shizuri clan|ja|倭文部|1=Shizuri clan|2=ja|3=倭文氏|4=zh|5=倭文氏|6=de|7=Shizuri Clan|8=fr|9=Clan Shizuri|lt=Shizuri Bemin (vassals of the Shizuri clan)|lt=Shizuri clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|qq=}}''', who worshipped Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto as their ancestral deity.
The '''origin of these shrines is attributed to''' the '''{{ill|Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine|ja|葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine|qq=}}''' in '''Katsuragi, Nara Prefecture'''. However, while the introduction and promotion of silk weaving technology in Japan is credited to '''{{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Nintoku|qq=}}''', some Shidori Shrines claim their origins date back to the reign of '''{{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Sujin|qq=}}'''. Therefore, these shrines provide '''important historical evidence''' regarding '''when and where silk weaving developed in Japan (Wa).'''
Additionally, in the '''2016 animated film "[[:en:Your_Name|Your Name]]"''', the fictional '''{{ill|Miyamizu Shrine|ja|宮水神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamizu Shrine|qq=}}''' is depicted as enshrining '''Shidori deity Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto (倭文神建葉槌命)'''
== Shizuri Shrines of the Engishiki ==
*'''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Isesaki)|ja|倭文神社 (伊勢崎市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Isesaki)|qq=}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Shidori-go, Nanba District, Kōzuke Province''' – (Higashi-Kaminomiya-cho, {{ill|Isesaki, Gunma|ja|伊勢崎市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isesaki, Gunma|qq=}}, [[Gunma Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shizu Shrine|ja|静神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizu Shrine|qq=}} (静神社)''' in '''Kuji District, Hitachi Province''' – ({{ill|Naka, Ibaraki|ja|那珂市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naka, Ibaraki|qq=}}, [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Nirasaki)|ja|倭文神社 (韮崎市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Nirasaki)|qq=}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Koma District, Kai Province''' – (Miyakubo, {{ill|Nirasaki, Yamanashi|ja|韮崎市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nirasaki, Yamanashi|qq=}}, [[Yamanashi Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Fujinomiya)|ja|倭文神社 (富士宮市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Fujinomiya)|qq=}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Fuji District, Suruga Province''' – (Hoshiyama, {{ill|Fujinomiya, Shizuoka|ja|富士宮市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujinomiya, Shizuoka|qq=}}, [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]) * '''Shidori Shrine (倭文神社)''' in '''Tagata District, Izu Province''' – Identified with multiple candidate shrines, including '''{{ill|Kuwado Shrine|ja|鍬戸神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuwado Shrine|qq=}}''' ({{ill|Mishima, Shizuoka|ja|三島市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima, Shizuoka|qq=}}, [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]) * '''Shidori Shrine (倭文神社)''' in '''Suzuka District, Ise Province''' – '''{{ill|Kasado Shrine|ja|加佐登神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasado Shrine|qq=}}''' (merged into [[Suzuka, Mie|Suzuka]], [[Mie Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Maizuru)|ja|倭文神社 (舞鶴市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Maizuru)|qq=}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Kasa District, Tango Province''' – (Imada, {{ill|Maizuru, Kyoto|ja|舞鶴市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maizuru, Kyoto|qq=}}, [[Kyoto Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Yosano)|ja|倭文神社 (与謝野町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Yosano)|qq=}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Yosa District, Tango Province''' – (Mikawachi, {{ill|Yosano, Kyoto|ja|与謝野町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yosano, Kyoto|qq=}}, [[Kyoto Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Asago)|ja|倭文神社 (朝来市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Asago)|qq=}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Asago District, Tajima Province''' – (Maruyama, {{ill|Asago, Hyogo|ja|朝来市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asago, Hyogo|qq=}}, [[Hyōgo Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Tottori)|ja|倭文神社 (鳥取市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Tottori)|qq=}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Takakusa District, Inaba Province''' – (Shidori, {{ill|Tottori, Tottori|ja|鳥取市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tottori, Tottori|qq=}}, [[Tottori Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|ja|倭文神社 (湯梨浜町)|1=Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|qq=}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Kawamura District, Hōki Province''' – (Miyauchi, {{ill|Yurihama, Tottori|ja|湯梨浜町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yurihama, Tottori|qq=}}, [[Tottori Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Kurayoshi)|ja|倭文神社 (倉吉市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Kurayoshi)|qq=}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Kume District, Hōki Province''' – (Shizu, {{ill|Kurayoshi, Tottori|ja|倉吉市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurayoshi, Tottori|qq=}}, [[Tottori Prefecture]])
== Other shrines == *'''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Tono)|ja|倭文神社 (遠野市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Tono)|qq=}}''' (Tsuchibuchi-cho, {{ill|Tono, Iwate|ja|遠野市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tono, Iwate|qq=}}, [[Iwate Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Nara)|ja|倭文神社 (奈良市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Nara)|qq=}}''' (Nishikujo-cho, {{ill|Nara, Nara|ja|奈良市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara, Nara|qq=}}, [[Nara Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Tsuyama)|ja|倭文神社 (津山市油木北)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Tsuyama)|qq=}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Shidori-go, Kume District, Mimasaka Province''' – (Yukikita, {{ill|Tsuyama, Okayama|ja|津山市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuyama, Okayama|qq=}}, [[Okayama Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Awaji)|ja|倭文神社 (あわじ市倭文委文)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Awaji)|qq=}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Shidori-go, Mihara District, Awaji Province''' – (Shidori, {{ill|Minamiawaji, Hyogo|ja|南あわじ市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamiawaji, Hyogo|qq=}}, [[Hyōgo Prefecture]])
Additionally, '''{{ill|Ōmika Shrine|8=ja|9=大甕神社|ja|大甕神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmika Shrine|qq=}}''' ({{ill|Hitachi, Ibaraki|ja|日立市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitachi, Ibaraki|qq=}}, [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]) also enshrines '''Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto'''. Similarly, '''{{ill|Ishii Shrine|ja|石井神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishii Shrine|qq=}}''' ({{ill|Kasama, Ibaraki|ja|笠間市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasama, Ibaraki|qq=}}, [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]) enshrines the same deity, with a large sacred rock called "Mitarai" located to the south. == See Also ==
* '''{{ill|Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}}''' (also known as Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto) * '''{{ill|Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|ja|天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}''' (also known as Amanoorihime)

revision.text:
'''Shidori Shrine''' (倭文神社) refers to Shinto shrines bearing the name "Shidori" (also read as Shizuri, Shitori, or Shidori).
These shrines enshrine '''{{ill|Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}}''' (also known as '''Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto''', '''Ameno Hachio''', or '''Takehatsuchi''') as the '''deity of weaving''', alongside '''{{ill|Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|ja|天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}''' (also known as '''Amano Yachichihime''' or '''Amanoorihime'''). These shrines were established by the '''{{ill|Shizuri clan|ja|倭文部|1=Shizuri clan|2=ja|3=倭文氏|4=zh|5=倭文氏|6=de|7=Shizuri Clan|8=fr|9=Clan Shizuri|lt=Shizuri Bemin (vassals of the Shizuri clan)|lt=Shizuri clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|qq=}}''', who worshipped Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto as their ancestral deity.
The '''origin of these shrines is attributed to''' the '''{{ill|Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine|ja|葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine|qq=}}''' in '''Katsuragi, Nara Prefecture'''. However, while the introduction and promotion of silk weaving technology in Japan is credited to '''{{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Nintoku|qq=}}''', some Shidori Shrines claim their origins date back to the reign of '''{{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Sujin|qq=}}'''. Therefore, these shrines provide '''important historical evidence''' regarding '''when and where silk weaving developed in Japan (Wa).'''
Additionally, in the '''2016 animated film "[[:en:Your_Name|Your Name]]"''', the fictional '''{{ill|Miyamizu Shrine|ja|宮水神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamizu Shrine|qq=}}''' is depicted as enshrining '''Shidori deity Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto (倭文神建葉槌命)'''
== Shizuri Shrines of the Engishiki ==
*'''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Isesaki)|ja|倭文神社 (伊勢崎市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Isesaki)|qq=}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Shidori-go, Nanba District, Kōzuke Province''' – (Higashi-Kaminomiya-cho, {{ill|Isesaki, Gunma|ja|伊勢崎市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isesaki, Gunma|qq=}}, [[Gunma Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shizu Shrine|ja|静神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizu Shrine|qq=}} (静神社)''' in '''Kuji District, Hitachi Province''' – ({{ill|Naka, Ibaraki|ja|那珂市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naka, Ibaraki|qq=}}, [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Nirasaki)|ja|倭文神社 (韮崎市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Nirasaki)|qq=}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Koma District, Kai Province''' – (Miyakubo, {{ill|Nirasaki, Yamanashi|ja|韮崎市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nirasaki, Yamanashi|qq=}}, [[Yamanashi Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Fujinomiya)|ja|倭文神社 (富士宮市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Fujinomiya)|qq=}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Fuji District, Suruga Province''' – (Hoshiyama, {{ill|Fujinomiya, Shizuoka|ja|富士宮市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujinomiya, Shizuoka|qq=}}, [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]) * '''Shidori Shrine (倭文神社)''' in '''Tagata District, Izu Province''' – Identified with multiple candidate shrines, including '''{{ill|Kuwado Shrine|ja|鍬戸神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuwado Shrine|qq=}}''' ({{ill|Mishima, Shizuoka|ja|三島市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima, Shizuoka|qq=}}, [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]) * '''Shidori Shrine (倭文神社)''' in '''Suzuka District, Ise Province''' – '''{{ill|Kasado Shrine|ja|加佐登神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasado Shrine|qq=}}''' (merged into [[Suzuka, Mie|Suzuka]], [[Mie Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Maizuru)|ja|倭文神社 (舞鶴市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Maizuru)|qq=}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Kasa District, Tango Province''' – (Imada, {{ill|Maizuru, Kyoto|ja|舞鶴市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maizuru, Kyoto|qq=}}, [[Kyoto Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Yosano)|ja|倭文神社 (与謝野町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Yosano)|qq=}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Yosa District, Tango Province''' – (Mikawachi, {{ill|Yosano, Kyoto|ja|与謝野町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yosano, Kyoto|qq=}}, [[Kyoto Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Asago)|ja|倭文神社 (朝来市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Asago)|qq=}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Asago District, Tajima Province''' – (Maruyama, {{ill|Asago, Hyōgo|ja|朝来市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asago, Hyōgo|qq=}}, [[Hyōgo Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Tottori)|ja|倭文神社 (鳥取市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Tottori)|qq=}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Takakusa District, Inaba Province''' – (Shidori, {{ill|Tottori, Tottori|ja|鳥取市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tottori, Tottori|qq=}}, [[Tottori Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|ja|倭文神社 (湯梨浜町)|1=Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|qq=}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Kawamura District, Hōki Province''' – (Miyauchi, {{ill|Yurihama, Tottori|ja|湯梨浜町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yurihama, Tottori|qq=}}, [[Tottori Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Kurayoshi)|ja|倭文神社 (倉吉市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Kurayoshi)|qq=}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Kume District, Hōki Province''' – (Shizu, {{ill|Kurayoshi, Tottori|ja|倉吉市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurayoshi, Tottori|qq=}}, [[Tottori Prefecture]])
== Other shrines == *'''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Tono)|ja|倭文神社 (遠野市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Tono)|qq=}}''' (Tsuchibuchi-cho, {{ill|Tono, Iwate|ja|遠野市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tono, Iwate|qq=}}, [[Iwate Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Nara)|ja|倭文神社 (奈良市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Nara)|qq=}}''' (Nishikujo-cho, {{ill|Nara, Nara|ja|奈良市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara, Nara|qq=}}, [[Nara Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Tsuyama)|ja|倭文神社 (津山市油木北)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Tsuyama)|qq=}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Shidori-go, Kume District, Mimasaka Province''' – (Yukikita, {{ill|Tsuyama, Okayama|ja|津山市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuyama, Okayama|qq=}}, [[Okayama Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Awaji)|ja|倭文神社 (あわじ市倭文委文)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Awaji)|qq=}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Shidori-go, Mihara District, Awaji Province''' – (Shidori, {{ill|Minamiawaji, Hyōgo|ja|南あわじ市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamiawaji, Hyōgo|qq=}}, [[Hyōgo Prefecture]])
Additionally, '''{{ill|Ōmika Shrine|8=ja|9=大甕神社|ja|大甕神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmika Shrine|qq=}}''' ({{ill|Hitachi, Ibaraki|ja|日立市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitachi, Ibaraki|qq=}}, [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]) also enshrines '''Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto'''. Similarly, '''{{ill|Ishii Shrine|ja|石井神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishii Shrine|qq=}}''' ({{ill|Kasama, Ibaraki|ja|笠間市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasama, Ibaraki|qq=}}, [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]) enshrines the same deity, with a large sacred rock called "Mitarai" located to the south. == See Also ==
* '''{{ill|Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}}''' (also known as Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto) * '''{{ill|Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|ja|天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}''' (also known as Amanoorihime)

'''Shidori Shrine''' (倭文神社) refers to Shinto shrines bearing the name "Shidori" (also read as Shizuri, Shitori, or Shidori).
These shrines enshrine '''{{ill|Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}}''' (also known as '''Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto''', '''Ameno Hachio''', or '''Takehatsuchi''') as the '''deity of weaving''', alongside '''{{ill|Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|ja|天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}''' (also known as '''Amano Yachichihime''' or '''Amanoorihime'''). These shrines were established by the '''{{ill|Shizuri clan|ja|倭文部|1=Shizuri clan|2=ja|3=倭文氏|4=zh|5=倭文氏|6=de|7=Shizuri Clan|8=fr|9=Clan Shizuri|lt=Shizuri Bemin (vassals of the Shizuri clan)|lt=Shizuri clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|qq=}}''', who worshipped Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto as their ancestral deity.
The '''origin of these shrines is attributed to''' the '''{{ill|Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine|ja|葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine|qq=}}''' in '''Katsuragi, Nara Prefecture'''. However, while the introduction and promotion of silk weaving technology in Japan is credited to '''[[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]''', some Shidori Shrines claim their origins date back to the reign of '''[[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]'''. Therefore, these shrines provide '''important historical evidence''' regarding '''when and where silk weaving developed in Japan (Wa).'''
Additionally, in the '''2016 animated film "[[:en:Your_Name|Your Name]]"''', the fictional '''{{ill|Miyamizu Shrine|ja|宮水神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamizu Shrine|qq=}}''' is depicted as enshrining '''Shidori deity Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto (倭文神建葉槌命)'''

(Miyauchi, [[:en:Yurihama, Tottori|Yurihama, Tottori]], [[Tottori Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Kurayoshi)|ja|倭文神社 (倉吉市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Kurayoshi)|qq=}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Kume District, Hōki Province''' – (Shizu, [[:en:Kurayoshi, Tottori|Kurayoshi, Tottori]], [[Tottori Prefecture]])
== Other shrines == *'''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Tono)|ja|倭文神社 (遠野市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Tono)|qq=}}''' (Tsuchibuchi-cho, [[:en:Tōno, Iwate|Tono, Iwate]], [[Iwate Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Nara)|ja|倭文神社 (奈良市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Nara)|qq=}}''' (Nishikujo-cho, [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara, Nara]], [[Nara Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Tsuyama)|ja|倭文神社 (津山市油木北)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Tsuyama)|qq=}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Shidori-go, Kume District, Mimasaka Province''' – (Yukikita, [[:en:Tsuyama|Tsuyama, Okayama]], [[Okayama Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Awaji)|ja|倭文神社 (あわじ市倭文委文)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Awaji)|qq=}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Shidori-go, Mihara District, Awaji Province''' – (Shidori, [[:en:Minamiawaji|Minamiawaji, Hyōgo]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]])
Additionally, '''{{ill|Ōmika Shrine|8=ja|9=大甕神社|ja|大甕神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmika Shrine|qq=}}''' ([[:en:Hitachi, Ibaraki|Hitachi, Ibaraki]], [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]) also enshrines '''Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto'''. Similarly, '''{{ill|Ishii Shrine|ja|石井神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishii Shrine|qq=}}''' ([[:en:Kasama, Ibaraki|Kasama, Ibaraki]], [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]) enshrines the same deity, with a large sacred rock called "Mitarai" located to the south. == See Also ==
* '''{{ill|Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}}''' (also known as Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto) * '''{{ill|Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|ja|天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}''' (also known as Amanoorihime)

revision.comment: Bot: append Wikidata-derived interwikis
revision.text:
'''Shidori Shrine''' (倭文神社) refers to Shinto shrines bearing the name "Shidori" (also read as Shizuri, Shitori, or Shidori).
These shrines enshrine '''{{ill|Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}}''' (also known as '''Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto''', '''Ameno Hachio''', or '''Takehatsuchi''') as the '''deity of weaving''', alongside '''{{ill|Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|ja|天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}''' (also known as '''Amano Yachichihime''' or '''Amanoorihime'''). These shrines were established by the '''{{ill|Shizuri clan|ja|倭文部|1=Shizuri clan|2=ja|3=倭文氏|4=zh|5=倭文氏|6=de|7=Shizuri Clan|8=fr|9=Clan Shizuri|lt=Shizuri Bemin (vassals of the Shizuri clan)|lt=Shizuri clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|qq=}}''', who worshipped Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto as their ancestral deity.
The '''origin of these shrines is attributed to''' the '''{{ill|Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine|ja|葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine|qq=}}''' in '''Katsuragi, Nara Prefecture'''. However, while the introduction and promotion of silk weaving technology in Japan is credited to '''[[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]''', some Shidori Shrines claim their origins date back to the reign of '''[[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]'''. Therefore, these shrines provide '''important historical evidence''' regarding '''when and where silk weaving developed in Japan (Wa).'''
Additionally, in the '''2016 animated film "[[:en:Your_Name|Your Name]]"''', the fictional '''{{ill|Miyamizu Shrine|ja|宮水神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamizu Shrine|qq=}}''' is depicted as enshrining '''Shidori deity Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto (倭文神建葉槌命)'''
== Shizuri Shrines of the Engishiki ==
*'''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Isesaki)|ja|倭文神社 (伊勢崎市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Isesaki)|qq=}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Shidori-go, Nanba District, Kōzuke Province''' – (Higashi-Kaminomiya-cho, [[:en:Isesaki|Isesaki, Gunma]], [[Gunma Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shizu Shrine|ja|静神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizu Shrine|qq=}} (静神社)''' in '''Kuji District, Hitachi Province''' – ([[:en:Naka, Ibaraki|Naka, Ibaraki]], [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Nirasaki)|ja|倭文神社 (韮崎市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Nirasaki)|qq=}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Koma District, Kai Province''' – (Miyakubo, [[:en:Nirasaki, Yamanashi|Nirasaki, Yamanashi]], [[Yamanashi Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Fujinomiya)|ja|倭文神社 (富士宮市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Fujinomiya)|qq=}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Fuji District, Suruga Province''' – (Hoshiyama, [[:en:Fujinomiya, Shizuoka|Fujinomiya, Shizuoka]], [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]) * '''Shidori Shrine (倭文神社)''' in '''Tagata District, Izu Province''' – Identified with multiple candidate shrines, including '''{{ill|Kuwado Shrine|ja|鍬戸神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuwado Shrine|qq=}}''' ([[:en:Mishima, Shizuoka|Mishima, Shizuoka]], [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]) * '''Shidori Shrine (倭文神社)''' in '''Suzuka District, Ise Province''' – '''{{ill|Kasado Shrine|ja|加佐登神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasado Shrine|qq=}}''' (merged into [[Suzuka, Mie|Suzuka]], [[Mie Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Maizuru)|ja|倭文神社 (舞鶴市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Maizuru)|qq=}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Kasa District, Tango Province''' – (Imada, [[:en:Maizuru|Maizuru, Kyoto]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Yosano)|ja|倭文神社 (与謝野町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Yosano)|qq=}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Yosa District, Tango Province''' – (Mikawachi, [[:en:Yosano, Kyoto|Yosano, Kyoto]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Asago)|ja|倭文神社 (朝来市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Asago)|qq=}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Asago District, Tajima Province''' – (Maruyama, [[:en:Asago, Hyōgo|Asago, Hyōgo]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Tottori)|ja|倭文神社 (鳥取市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Tottori)|qq=}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Takakusa District, Inaba Province''' – (Shidori, [[:en:Tottori (city)|Tottori, Tottori]], [[Tottori Prefecture]]) * '''[[:en:Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|Shitori Shrine (Tottori)]] (倭文神社)''' in '''Kawamura District, Hōki Province''' – (Miyauchi, [[:en:Yurihama, Tottori|Yurihama, Tottori]], [[Tottori Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Kurayoshi)|ja|倭文神社 (倉吉市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Kurayoshi)|qq=}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Kume District, Hōki Province''' – (Shizu, [[:en:Kurayoshi, Tottori|Kurayoshi, Tottori]], [[Tottori Prefecture]])
== Other shrines == *'''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Tono)|ja|倭文神社 (遠野市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Tono)|qq=}}''' (Tsuchibuchi-cho, [[:en:Tōno, Iwate|Tono, Iwate]], [[Iwate Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Nara)|ja|倭文神社 (奈良市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Nara)|qq=}}''' (Nishikujo-cho, [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara, Nara]], [[Nara Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Tsuyama)|ja|倭文神社 (津山市油木北)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Tsuyama)|qq=}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Shidori-go, Kume District, Mimasaka Province''' – (Yukikita, [[:en:Tsuyama|Tsuyama, Okayama]], [[Okayama Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Awaji)|ja|倭文神社 (あわじ市倭文委文)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Awaji)|qq=}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Shidori-go, Mihara District, Awaji Province''' – (Shidori, [[:en:Minamiawaji|Minamiawaji, Hyōgo]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]])
Additionally, '''{{ill|Ōmika Shrine|8=ja|9=大甕神社|ja|大甕神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmika Shrine|qq=}}''' ([[:en:Hitachi, Ibaraki|Hitachi, Ibaraki]], [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]) also enshrines '''Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto'''. Similarly, '''{{ill|Ishii Shrine|ja|石井神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishii Shrine|qq=}}''' ([[:en:Kasama, Ibaraki|Kasama, Ibaraki]], [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]) enshrines the same deity, with a large sacred rock called "Mitarai" located to the south. == See Also ==
* '''{{ill|Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}}''' (also known as Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto) * '''{{ill|Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|ja|天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}''' (also known as Amanoorihime)

revision.comment: Bot: remove deleted category Imported Shinto cults
revision.text:
'''Shidori Shrine''' (倭文神社) refers to Shinto shrines bearing the name "Shidori" (also read as Shizuri, Shitori, or Shidori).
These shrines enshrine '''{{ill|Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}}''' (also known as '''Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto''', '''Ameno Hachio''', or '''Takehatsuchi''') as the '''deity of weaving''', alongside '''{{ill|Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|ja|天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}''' (also known as '''Amano Yachichihime''' or '''Amanoorihime'''). These shrines were established by the '''{{ill|Shizuri clan|ja|倭文部|1=Shizuri clan|2=ja|3=倭文氏|4=zh|5=倭文氏|6=de|7=Shizuri Clan|8=fr|9=Clan Shizuri|lt=Shizuri Bemin (vassals of the Shizuri clan)|lt=Shizuri clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|qq=}}''', who worshipped Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto as their ancestral deity.
The '''origin of these shrines is attributed to''' the '''{{ill|Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine|ja|葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine|qq=}}''' in '''Katsuragi, Nara Prefecture'''. However, while the introduction and promotion of silk weaving technology in Japan is credited to '''[[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]''', some Shidori Shrines claim their origins date back to the reign of '''[[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]'''. Therefore, these shrines provide '''important historical evidence''' regarding '''when and where silk weaving developed in Japan (Wa).'''
Additionally, in the '''2016 animated film "[[:en:Your_Name|Your Name]]"''', the fictional '''{{ill|Miyamizu Shrine|ja|宮水神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamizu Shrine|qq=}}''' is depicted as enshrining '''Shidori deity Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto (倭文神建葉槌命)'''
== Shizuri Shrines of the Engishiki ==
*'''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Isesaki)|ja|倭文神社 (伊勢崎市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Isesaki)|qq=}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Shidori-go, Nanba District, Kōzuke Province''' – (Higashi-Kaminomiya-cho, [[:en:Isesaki|Isesaki, Gunma]], [[Gunma Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shizu Shrine|ja|静神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizu Shrine|qq=}} (静神社)''' in '''Kuji District, Hitachi Province''' – ([[:en:Naka, Ibaraki|Naka, Ibaraki]], [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Nirasaki)|ja|倭文神社 (韮崎市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Tottori|Kurayoshi, Tottori]], [[Tottori Prefecture]])
== Other shrines == *'''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Tono)|ja|倭文神社 (遠野市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Tono)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (Tsuchibuchi-cho, [[:en:Tōno, Iwate|Tono, Iwate]], [[Iwate Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Nara)|ja|倭文神社 (奈良市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Nara)|qq=}}''' (Nishikujo-cho, [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara, Nara]], [[Nara Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Tsuyama)|ja|倭文神社 (津山市油木北)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Tsuyama)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Shidori-go, Kume District, Mimasaka Province''' – (Yukikita, [[:en:Tsuyama|Tsuyama, Okayama]], [[Okayama Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Awaji)|ja|倭文神社 (あわじ市倭文委文)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Awaji)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Shidori-go, Mihara District, Awaji Province''' – (Shidori, [[:en:Minamiawaji|Minamiawaji, Hyōgo]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]])
