revision.text:
'''Shidori Shrine''' (倭文神社) refers to Shinto shrines bearing the name "Shidori" (also read as Shizuri, Shitori, or Shidori).
These shrines enshrine '''{{ill|Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}}''' (also known as '''Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto''', '''Ameno Hachio''', or '''Takehatsuchi''') as the '''deity of weaving''', alongside '''{{ill|Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|ja|天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}''' (also known as '''Amano Yachichihime''' or '''Amanoorihime'''). These shrines were established by the '''{{ill|Shizuri clan|ja|倭文部|1=Shizuri clan|2=ja|3=倭文氏|4=zh|5=倭文氏|6=de|7=Shizuri Clan|8=fr|9=Clan Shizuri|lt=Shizuri Bemin (vassals of the Shizuri clan)|lt=Shizuri clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|qq=}}''', who worshipped Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto as their ancestral deity.
The '''origin of these shrines is attributed to''' the '''{{ill|Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine|ja|葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine|qq=}}''' in '''Katsuragi, Nara Prefecture'''. However, while the introduction and promotion of silk weaving technology in Japan is credited to '''[[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]''', some Shidori Shrines claim their origins date back to the reign of '''[[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]'''. Therefore, these shrines provide '''important historical evidence''' regarding '''when and where silk weaving developed in Japan (Wa).'''
Additionally, in the '''2016 animated film "[[:en:Your_Name|Your Name]]"''', the fictional '''{{ill|Miyamizu Shrine|ja|宮水神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamizu Shrine|qq=}}''' is depicted as enshrining '''Shidori deity Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto (倭文神建葉槌命)'''
== Shizuri Shrines of the Engishiki ==
*'''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Isesaki)|ja|倭文神社 (伊勢崎市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Isesaki)|qq=}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Shidori-go, Nanba District, Kōzuke Province''' – (Higashi-Kaminomiya-cho, [[:en:Isesaki|Isesaki, Gunma]], [[Gunma Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shizu Shrine|ja|静神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizu Shrine|qq=}} (静神社)''' in '''Kuji District, Hitachi Province''' – ([[:en:Naka, Ibaraki|Naka, Ibaraki]], [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Nirasaki)|ja|倭文神社 (韮崎市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Nirasaki)|qq=}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Koma District, Kai Province''' – (Miyakubo, [[:en:Nirasaki, Yamanashi|Nirasaki, Yamanashi]], [[Yamanashi Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Fujinomiya)|ja|倭文神社 (富士宮市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Fujinomiya)|qq=}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Fuji District, Suruga Province''' – (Hoshiyama, [[:en:Fujinomiya, Shizuoka|Fujinomiya, Shizuoka]], [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]) * '''Shidori Shrine (倭文神社)''' in '''Tagata District, Izu Province''' – Identified with multiple candidate shrines, including '''{{ill|Kuwado Shrine|ja|鍬戸神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuwado Shrine|qq=}}''' ([[:en:Mishima, Shizuoka|Mishima, Shizuoka]], [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]) * '''Shidori Shrine (倭文神社)''' in '''Suzuka District, Ise Province''' – '''{{ill|Kasado Shrine|ja|加佐登神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasado Shrine|qq=}}''' (merged into [[Suzuka, Mie|Suzuka]], [[Mie Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Maizuru)|ja|倭文神社 (舞鶴市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Maizuru)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Kasa District, Tango Province''' – (Imada, [[:en:Maizuru|Maizuru, Kyoto]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Yosano)|ja|倭文神社 (与謝野町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Yosano)|qq=}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Yosa District, Tango Province''' – (Mikawachi, [[:en:Yosano, Kyoto|Yosano, Kyoto]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Asago)|ja|倭文神社 (朝来市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Asago)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Asago District, Tajima Province''' – (Maruyama, [[:en:Asago, Hyōgo|Asago, Hyōgo]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Tottori)|ja|倭文神社 (鳥取市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Tottori)|qq=}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Takakusa District, Inaba Province''' – (Shidori, [[:en:Tottori (city)|Tottori, Tottori]], [[Tottori Prefecture]]) * '''[[:en:Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|Shitori Shrine (Tottori)]] (倭文神社)''' in '''Kawamura District, Hōki Province''' – (Miyauchi, [[:en:Yurihama, Tottori|Yurihama, Tottori]], [[Tottori Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Kurayoshi)|ja|倭文神社 (倉吉市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Kurayoshi)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Kume District, Hōki Province''' – (Shizu, [[:en:Kurayoshi, Tottori|Kurayoshi, Tottori]], [[Tottori Prefecture]])
== Other shrines == *'''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Tono)|ja|倭文神社 (遠野市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Tono)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (Tsuchibuchi-cho, [[:en:Tōno, Iwate|Tono, Iwate]], [[Iwate Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Nara)|ja|倭文神社 (奈良市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Nara)|qq=}}''' (Nishikujo-cho, [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara, Nara]], [[Nara Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Tsuyama)|ja|倭文神社 (津山市油木北)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Tsuyama)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Shidori-go, Kume District, Mimasaka Province''' – (Yukikita, [[:en:Tsuyama|Tsuyama, Okayama]], [[Okayama Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Awaji)|ja|倭文神社 (あわじ市倭文委文)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Awaji)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Shidori-go, Mihara District, Awaji Province''' – (Shidori, [[:en:Minamiawaji|Minamiawaji, Hyōgo]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]])
Additionally, '''{{ill|Ōmika Shrine|8=ja|9=大甕神社|ja|大甕神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmika Shrine|qq=}}''' ([[:en:Hitachi, Ibaraki|Hitachi, Ibaraki]], [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]) also enshrines '''Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto'''. Similarly, '''{{ill|Ishii Shrine|ja|石井神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishii Shrine|qq=}}''' ([[:en:Kasama, Ibaraki|Kasama, Ibaraki]], [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]) enshrines the same deity, with a large sacred rock called "Mitarai" located to the south. == See Also ==
* '''{{ill|Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}}''' (also known as Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto) * '''{{ill|Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|ja|天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}''' (also known as Amanoorihime)

These shrines enshrine '''{{ill|Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}}''' (also known as '''Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto''', '''Ameno Hachio''', or '''Takehatsuchi''') as the '''deity of weaving''', alongside '''{{ill|Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|ja|天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}''' (also known as '''Amano Yachichihime''' or '''Amanoorihime'''). These shrines were established by the '''{{ill|Shizuri clan|ja|倭文部|1=Shizuri clan|2=ja|3=倭文氏|4=zh|5=倭文氏|6=de|7=Shizuri Clan|8=fr|9=Clan Shizuri|lt=Shizuri Bemin (vassals of the Shizuri clan)|lt=Shizuri clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|qq=}}''', who worshipped Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto as their ancestral deity.

However, while the introduction and promotion of silk weaving technology in Japan is credited to '''[[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]''', some Shidori Shrines claim their origins date back to the reign of '''[[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]'''. Additionally, '''{{ill|Ōmika Shrine|8=ja|9=大甕神社|ja|大甕神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmika Shrine|qq=}}''' ([[:en:Hitachi, Ibaraki|Hitachi, Ibaraki]], [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]) also enshrines '''Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto'''. Similarly, '''{{ill|Ishii Shrine|ja|石井神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishii Shrine|qq=}}''' ([[:en:Kasama, Ibaraki|Kasama, Ibaraki]], [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]) enshrines the same deity, with a large sacred rock called "Mitarai" located to the south. == See Also ==
* '''{{ill|Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}}''' (also known as Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto) * '''{{ill|Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|ja|天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}''' (also known as Amanoorihime)

revision.comment: Bot: remove deleted category Weaving and textiles in Japan
revision.text:
'''Shidori Shrine''' (倭文神社) refers to Shinto shrines bearing the name "Shidori" (also read as Shizuri, Shitori, or Shidori).
These shrines enshrine '''{{ill|Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}}''' (also known as '''Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto''', '''Ameno Hachio''', or '''Takehatsuchi''') as the '''deity of weaving''', alongside '''{{ill|Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|ja|天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}''' (also known as '''Amano Yachichihime''' or '''Amanoorihime'''). These shrines were established by the '''{{ill|Shizuri clan|ja|倭文部|1=Shizuri clan|2=ja|3=倭文氏|4=zh|5=倭文氏|6=de|7=Shizuri Clan|8=fr|9=Clan Shizuri|lt=Shizuri Bemin (vassals of the Shizuri clan)|lt=Shizuri clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|qq=}}''', who worshipped Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto as their ancestral deity.
The '''origin of these shrines is attributed to''' the '''{{ill|Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine|ja|葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine|qq=}}''' in '''Katsuragi, Nara Prefecture'''. However, while the introduction and promotion of silk weaving technology in Japan is credited to '''[[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]''', some Shidori Shrines claim their origins date back to the reign of '''[[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]'''. Therefore, these shrines provide '''important historical evidence''' regarding '''when and where silk weaving developed in Japan (Wa).'''
Additionally, in the '''2016 animated film "[[:en:Your_Name|Your Name]]"''', the fictional '''{{ill|Miyamizu Shrine|ja|宮水神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamizu Shrine|qq=}}''' is depicted as enshrining '''Shidori deity Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto (倭文神建葉槌命)'''
== Shizuri Shrines of the Engishiki ==
*'''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Isesaki)|ja|倭文神社 (伊勢崎市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Isesaki)|qq=}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Shidori-go, Nanba District, Kōzuke Province''' – (Higashi-Kaminomiya-cho, [[:en:Isesaki|Isesaki, Gunma]], [[Gunma Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shizu Shrine|ja|静神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizu Shrine|qq=}} (静神社)''' in '''Kuji District, Hitachi Province''' – ([[:en:Naka, Ibaraki|Naka, Ibaraki]], [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Nirasaki)|ja|倭文神社 (韮崎市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Nirasaki)|qq=}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Koma District, Kai Province''' – (Miyakubo, [[:en:Nirasaki, Yamanashi|Nirasaki, Yamanashi]], [[Yamanashi Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Fujinomiya)|ja|倭文神社 (富士宮市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Fujinomiya)|qq=}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Fuji District, Suruga Province''' – (Hoshiyama, [[:en:Fujinomiya, Shizuoka|Fujinomiya, Shizuoka]], [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]) * '''Shidori Shrine (倭文神社)''' in '''Tagata District, Izu Province''' – Identified with multiple candidate shrines, including '''{{ill|Kuwado Shrine|ja|鍬戸神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuwado Shrine|qq=}}''' ([[:en:Mishima, Shizuoka|Mishima, Shizuoka]], [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]) * '''Shidori Shrine (倭文神社)''' in '''Suzuka District, Ise Province''' – '''{{ill|Kasado Shrine|ja|加佐登神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasado Shrine|qq=}}''' (merged into [[Suzuka, Mie|Suzuka]], [[Mie Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Maizuru)|ja|倭文神社 (舞鶴市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Maizuru)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Kasa District, Tango Province''' – (Imada, [[:en:Maizuru|Maizuru, Kyoto]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Yosano)|ja|倭文神社 (与謝野町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Yosano)|qq=}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Yosa District, Tango Province''' – (Mikawachi, [[:en:Yosano, Kyoto|Yosano, Kyoto]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Asago)|ja|倭文神社 (朝来市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Asago)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Asago District, Tajima Province''' – (Maruyama, [[:en:Asago, Hyōgo|Asago, Hyōgo]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Tottori)|ja|倭文神社 (鳥取市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Tottori)|qq=}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Takakusa District, Inaba Province''' – (Shidori, [[:en:Tottori (city)|Tottori, Tottori]], [[Tottori Prefecture]]) * '''[[:en:Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|Shitori Shrine (Tottori)]] (倭文神社)''' in '''Kawamura District, Hōki Province''' – (Miyauchi, [[:en:Yurihama, Tottori|Yurihama, Tottori]], [[Tottori Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Kurayoshi)|ja|倭文神社 (倉吉市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Kurayoshi)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Kume District, Hōki Province''' – (Shizu, [[:en:Kurayoshi, Tottori|Kurayoshi, Tottori]], [[Tottori Prefecture]])
== Other shrines == *'''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Tono)|ja|倭文神社 (遠野市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Tono)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (Tsuchibuchi-cho, [[:en:Tōno, Iwate|Tono, Iwate]], [[Iwate Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Nara)|ja|倭文神社 (奈良市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Nara)|qq=}}''' (Nishikujo-cho, [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara, Nara]], [[Nara Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Tsuyama)|ja|倭文神社 (津山市油木北)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Tsuyama)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Shidori-go, Kume District, Mimasaka Province''' – (Yukikita, [[:en:Tsuyama|Tsuyama, Okayama]], [[Okayama Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Awaji)|ja|倭文神社 (あわじ市倭文委文)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Awaji)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Shidori-go, Mihara District, Awaji Province''' – (Shidori, [[:en:Minamiawaji|Minamiawaji, Hyōgo]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]])
Additionally, '''{{ill|Ōmika Shrine|8=ja|9=大甕神社|ja|大甕神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmika Shrine|qq=}}''' ([[:en:Hitachi, Ibaraki|Hitachi, Ibaraki]], [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]) also enshrines '''Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto'''. Similarly, '''{{ill|Ishii Shrine|ja|石井神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishii Shrine|qq=}}''' ([[:en:Kasama, Ibaraki|Kasama, Ibaraki]], [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]) enshrines the same deity, with a large sacred rock called "Mitarai" located to the south. == See Also ==
* '''{{ill|Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}}''' (also known as Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto) * '''{{ill|Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|ja|天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}''' (also known as Amanoorihime)

Similarly, '''{{ill|Ishii Shrine|ja|石井神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishii Shrine|qq=}}''' ([[:en:Kasama, Ibaraki|Kasama, Ibaraki]], [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]) enshrines the same deity, with a large sacred rock called "Mitarai" located to the south. == See Also ==

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
'''Shidori Shrine''' (倭文神社) refers to Shinto shrines bearing the name "Shidori" (also read as Shizuri, Shitori, or Shidori).
These shrines enshrine '''{{ill|Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}}''' (also known as '''Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto''', '''Ameno Hachio''', or '''Takehatsuchi''') as the '''deity of weaving''', alongside '''{{ill|Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|ja|天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}''' (also known as '''Amano Yachichihime''' or '''Amanoorihime'''). These shrines were established by the '''{{ill|Shizuri clan|ja|倭文部|1=Shizuri clan|2=ja|3=倭文氏|4=zh|5=倭文氏|6=de|7=Shizuri Clan|8=fr|9=Clan Shizuri|lt=Shizuri Bemin (vassals of the Shizuri clan)|lt=Shizuri clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|qq=}}''', who worshipped Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto as their ancestral deity.
The '''origin of these shrines is attributed to''' the '''{{ill|Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine|ja|葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine|qq=}}''' in '''Katsuragi, Nara Prefecture'''. However, while the introduction and promotion of silk weaving technology in Japan is credited to '''[[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]''', some Shidori Shrines claim their origins date back to the reign of '''[[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]'''. Therefore, these shrines provide '''important historical evidence''' regarding '''when and where silk weaving developed in Japan (Wa).'''
Additionally, in the '''2016 animated film "[[:en:Your_Name|Your Name]]"''', the fictional '''{{ill|Miyamizu Shrine|ja|宮水神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamizu Shrine|qq=}}''' is depicted as enshrining '''Shidori deity Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto (倭文神建葉槌命)'''
== Shizuri Shrines of the Engishiki ==
*'''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Isesaki)|ja|倭文神社 (伊勢崎市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Isesaki)|qq=}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Shidori-go, Nanba District, Kōzuke Province''' – (Higashi-Kaminomiya-cho, [[:en:Isesaki|Isesaki, Gunma]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shizu Shrine|ja|静神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizu Shrine|qq=}} (静神社)''' in '''Kuji District, Hitachi Province''' – ([[:en:Naka, Ibaraki|Naka, Ibaraki]], [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Nirasaki)|ja|倭文神社 [[Yamanashi Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Fujinomiya)|ja|倭文神社 (富士宮市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Fujinomiya)|qq=}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Fuji District, Suruga Province''' – (Hoshiyama, [[:en:Fujinomiya, Shizuoka|Fujinomiya, Shizuoka]], [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]) * '''Shidori Shrine (倭文神社)''' in '''Tagata District, Izu Province''' – Identified with multiple candidate shrines, including '''{{ill|Kuwado Shrine|ja|鍬戸神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuwado Shrine|qq=}}''' ([[:en:Mishima, Shizuoka|Mishima, Shizuoka]], [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]) * '''Shidori Shrine (倭文神社)''' in '''Suzuka District, Ise Province''' – '''{{ill|Kasado Shrine|ja|加佐登神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasado Shrine|qq=}}''' (merged into [[Suzuka, Mie|Suzuka]], [[Mie Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Maizuru)|ja|倭文神社 (舞鶴市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Maizuru)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Kasa District, Tango Province''' – (Imada, [[:en:Maizuru|Maizuru, Kyoto]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Yosano)|ja|倭文神社 (与謝野町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Yosano)|qq=}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Yosa District, Tango Province''' – (Mikawachi, [[:en:Yosano, Kyoto|Yosano, Kyoto]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Asago)|ja|倭文神社 (朝来市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Asago)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Asago District, Tajima Province''' – (Maruyama, [[:en:Asago, Hyōgo|Asago, Hyōgo]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Tottori)|ja|倭文神社 (鳥取市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Tottori)|qq=}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Takakusa District, Inaba Province''' – (Shidori, [[:en:Tottori (city)|Tottori, Tottori]], [[Tottori Prefecture]]) * '''[[:en:Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|Shitori Shrine (Tottori)]] (倭文神社)''' in '''Kawamura District, Hōki Province''' – (Miyauchi, [[:en:Yurihama, Tottori|Yurihama, Tottori]], [[Tottori Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Kurayoshi)|ja|倭文神社 (倉吉市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Kurayoshi)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Kume District, Hōki Province''' – (Shizu, [[:en:Kurayoshi, Tottori|Kurayoshi, Tottori]], [[Tottori Prefecture]])
== Other shrines == *'''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Tono)|ja|倭文神社 (遠野市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Tono)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (Tsuchibuchi-cho, [[:en:Tōno, Iwate|Tono, Iwate]], [[Iwate Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Nara)|ja|倭文神社 (奈良市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Nara)|qq=}}''' (Nishikujo-cho, [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara, Nara]], [[Nara Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Tsuyama)|ja|倭文神社 (津山市油木北)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Tsuyama)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Shidori-go, Kume District, Mimasaka Province''' – (Yukikita, [[:en:Tsuyama|Tsuyama, Okayama]], [[Okayama Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Awaji)|ja|倭文神社 (あわじ市倭文委文)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Awaji)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Shidori-go, Mihara District, Awaji Province''' – (Shidori, [[:en:Minamiawaji|Minamiawaji, Hyōgo]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]])
Additionally, '''{{ill|Ōmika Shrine|8=ja|9=大甕神社|ja|大甕神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmika Shrine|qq=}}''' ([[:en:Hitachi, Ibaraki|Hitachi, Ibaraki]], [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]) also enshrines '''Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto'''. Similarly, '''{{ill|Ishii Shrine|ja|石井神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishii Shrine|qq=}}''' ([[:en:Kasama, Ibaraki|Kasama, Ibaraki]], [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]) enshrines the same deity, with a large sacred rock called "Mitarai" located to the south. == See Also ==

revision.text:
'''Shidori Shrine''' (倭文神社) refers to Shinto shrines bearing the name "Shidori" (also read as Shizuri, Shitori, or Shidori).
These shrines enshrine '''{{ill|Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}}''' (also known as '''Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto''', '''Ameno Hachio''', or '''Takehatsuchi''') as the '''deity of weaving''', alongside '''{{ill|Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|ja|天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}''' (also known as '''Amano Yachichihime''' or '''Amanoorihime'''). These shrines were established by the '''{{ill|Shizuri clan|ja|倭文部|1=Shizuri clan|2=ja|3=倭文氏|4=zh|5=倭文氏|6=de|7=Shizuri Clan|8=fr|9=Clan Shizuri|lt=Shizuri Bemin (vassals of the Shizuri clan)|lt=Shizuri clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|qq=}}''', who worshipped Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto as their ancestral deity.
The '''origin of these shrines is attributed to''' the '''{{ill|Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine|ja|葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine|qq=}}''' in '''Katsuragi, Nara Prefecture'''. However, while the introduction and promotion of silk weaving technology in Japan is credited to '''[[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]''', some Shidori Shrines claim their origins date back to the reign of '''[[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]'''. Therefore, these shrines provide '''important historical evidence''' regarding '''when and where silk weaving developed in Japan (Wa).'''
Additionally, in the '''2016 animated film "[[:en:Your_Name|Your Name]]"''', the fictional '''{{ill|Miyamizu Shrine|ja|宮水神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamizu Shrine|qq=}}''' is depicted as enshrining '''Shidori deity Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto (倭文神建葉槌命)'''
== Shizuri Shrines of the Engishiki ==
*'''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Isesaki)|ja|倭文神社 (伊勢崎市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Isesaki)|qq=}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Shidori-go, Nanba District, Kōzuke Province''' – (Higashi-Kaminomiya-cho, [[:en:Isesaki|Isesaki, Gunma]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shizu Shrine|ja|静神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizu Shrine|qq=}} (静神社)''' in '''Kuji District, Hitachi Province''' – ([[:en:Naka, Ibaraki|Naka, Ibaraki]], [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Nirasaki)|ja|倭文神社 (韮崎市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Nirasaki)|qq=}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Koma District, Kai Province''' – (Miyakubo, [[:en:Nirasaki, Yamanashi|Nirasaki, Yamanashi]], [[Yamanashi Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Fujinomiya)|ja|倭文神社 (富士宮市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Fujinomiya)|qq=}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Fuji District, Suruga Province''' – (Hoshiyama, [[:en:Fujinomiya, Shizuoka|Fujinomiya, Shizuoka]], [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]) * '''Shidori Shrine (倭文神社)''' in '''Tagata District, Izu Province''' – Identified with multiple candidate shrines, including '''{{ill|Kuwado Shrine|ja|鍬戸神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuwado Shrine|qq=}}''' ([[:en:Mishima, Shizuoka|Mishima, Shizuoka]], [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]) * '''Shidori Shrine (倭文神社)''' in '''Suzuka District, Ise Province''' – '''{{ill|Kasado Shrine|ja|加佐登神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasado Shrine|qq=}}''' (merged into [[Suzuka, Mie|Suzuka]], [[Mie Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Maizuru)|ja|倭文神社 (舞鶴市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Maizuru)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Kasa District, Tango Province''' – (Imada, [[:en:Maizuru|Maizuru, Kyoto]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Yosano)|ja|倭文神社 (与謝野町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Yosano)|qq=}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Yosa District, Tango Province''' – (Mikawachi, [[:en:Yosano, Kyoto|Yosano, Kyoto]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Asago)|ja|倭文神社 (朝来市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Asago)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Asago District, Tajima Province''' – (Maruyama, [[:en:Asago, Hyōgo|Asago, Hyōgo]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Tottori)|ja|倭文神社 (鳥取市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Tottori)|qq=}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Takakusa District, Inaba Province''' – (Shidori, [[:en:Tottori (city)|Tottori, Tottori]], [[Tottori Prefecture]]) * '''[[:en:Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|Shitori Shrine (Tottori)]] (倭文神社)''' in '''Kawamura District, Hōki Province''' – (Miyauchi, [[:en:Yurihama, Tottori|Yurihama, Tottori]], [[Tottori Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Kurayoshi)|ja|倭文神社 (倉吉市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Kurayoshi)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Kume District, Hōki Province''' – (Shizu, [[:en:Kurayoshi, Tottori|Kurayoshi, Tottori]], [[Tottori Prefecture]])
== Other shrines == *'''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Tono)|ja|倭文神社 (遠野市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Tono)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (Tsuchibuchi-cho, [[:en:Tōno, Iwate|Tono, Iwate]], [[Iwate Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Nara)|ja|倭文神社 (奈良市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Nara)|qq=}}''' (Nishikujo-cho, [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara, Nara]], [[Nara Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Tsuyama)|ja|倭文神社 (津山市油木北)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Tsuyama)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Shidori-go, Kume District, Mimasaka Province''' – (Yukikita, [[:en:Tsuyama|Tsuyama, Okayama]], [[Okayama Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Awaji)|ja|倭文神社 (あわじ市倭文委文)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Awaji)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Shidori-go, Mihara District, Awaji Province''' – (Shidori, [[:en:Minamiawaji|Minamiawaji, Hyōgo]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]])
Additionally, '''{{ill|Ōmika Shrine|8=ja|9=大甕神社|ja|大甕神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmika Shrine|qq=}}''' ([[:en:Hitachi, Ibaraki|Hitachi, Ibaraki]], [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]]) also enshrines '''Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto'''. Similarly, '''{{ill|Ishii Shrine|ja|石井神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishii Shrine|qq=}}''' ([[:en:Kasama, Ibaraki|Kasama, Ibaraki]], [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]]) enshrines the same deity, with a large sacred rock called "Mitarai" located to the south. == See Also ==
* '''{{ill|Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}}''' (also known as Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto) * '''{{ill|Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|ja|天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}''' (also known as Amanoorihime)

'''Shidori Shrine''' (倭文神社) refers to Shinto shrines bearing the name "Shidori" (also read as Shizuri, Shitori, or Shidori).
These shrines enshrine '''{{ill|Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}}''' (also known as '''Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto''', '''Ameno Hachio''', or '''Takehatsuchi''') as the '''deity of weaving''', alongside '''{{ill|Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|ja|天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}''' (also known as '''Amano Yachichihime''' or '''Amanoorihime'''). These shrines were established by the '''{{ill|Shizuri clan|ja|倭文部|1=Shizuri clan|2=ja|3=倭文氏|4=zh|5=倭文氏|6=de|7=Shizuri Clan|8=fr|9=Clan Shizuri|lt=Shizuri Bemin (vassals of the Shizuri clan)|lt=Shizuri clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|qq=}}''', who worshipped Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto as their ancestral deity.
The '''origin of these shrines is attributed to''' the '''{{ill|Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine|ja|葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine|qq=}}''' in '''Katsuragi, Nara Prefecture'''. However, while the introduction and promotion of silk weaving technology in Japan is credited to '''[[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]''', some Shidori Shrines claim their origins date back to the reign of '''[[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]'''. Therefore, these shrines provide '''important historical evidence''' regarding '''when and where silk weaving developed in Japan (Wa).'''
Additionally, in the '''2016 animated film "[[:en:Your_Name|Your Name]]"''', the fictional '''{{ill|Miyamizu Shrine|ja|宮水神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamizu Shrine|qq=}}''' is depicted as enshrining '''Shidori deity Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto (倭文神建葉槌命)'''

== Other shrines == *'''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Tono)|ja|倭文神社 (遠野市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Tono)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (Tsuchibuchi-cho, [[:en:Tōno, Iwate|Tono, Iwate]], [[Iwate Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Nara)|ja|倭文神社 (奈良市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Nara)|qq=}}''' (Nishikujo-cho, [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara, Nara]], [[Nara Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Tsuyama)|ja|倭文神社 (津山市油木北)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Tsuyama)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Shidori-go, Kume District, Mimasaka Province''' – (Yukikita, [[:en:Tsuyama|Tsuyama, Okayama]], [[Okayama Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Awaji)|ja|倭文神社 (あわじ市倭文委文)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Awaji)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Shidori-go, Mihara District, Awaji Province''' – (Shidori, [[:en:Minamiawaji|Minamiawaji, Hyōgo]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]])
Additionally, '''{{ill|Ōmika Shrine|8=ja|9=大甕神社|ja|大甕神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmika Shrine|qq=}}''' ([[:en:Hitachi, Ibaraki|Hitachi, Ibaraki]], [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]]) also enshrines '''Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto'''. Similarly, '''{{ill|Ishii Shrine|ja|石井神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishii Shrine|qq=}}''' ([[:en:Kasama, Ibaraki|Kasama, Ibaraki]], [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]]) enshrines the same deity, with a large sacred rock called "Mitarai" located to the south. == See Also ==
* '''{{ill|Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}}''' (also known as Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto) * '''{{ill|Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|ja|天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}''' (also known as Amanoorihime)

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
'''Shidori Shrine''' (倭文神社) refers to Shinto shrines bearing the name "Shidori" (also read as Shizuri, Shitori, or Shidori).
These shrines enshrine '''{{ill|Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}}''' (also known as '''Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto''', '''Ameno Hachio''', or '''Takehatsuchi''') as the '''deity of weaving''', alongside '''{{ill|Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|ja|天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}''' (also known as '''Amano Yachichihime''' or '''Amanoorihime'''). These shrines were established by the '''{{ill|Shizuri clan|ja|倭文部|1=Shizuri clan|2=ja|3=倭文氏|4=zh|5=倭文氏|6=de|7=Shizuri Clan|8=fr|9=Clan Shizuri|lt=Shizuri Bemin (vassals of the Shizuri clan)|lt=Shizuri clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|qq=}}''', who worshipped Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto as their ancestral deity.
The '''origin of these shrines is attributed to''' the '''{{ill|Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine|ja|葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine|qq=}}''' in '''Katsuragi, Nara Prefecture'''. However, while the introduction and promotion of silk weaving technology in Japan is credited to '''[[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]''', some Shidori Shrines claim their origins date back to the reign of '''[[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]'''. Therefore, these shrines provide '''important historical evidence''' regarding '''when and where silk weaving developed in Japan (Wa).'''
Additionally, in the '''2016 animated film "[[:en:Your_Name|Your Name]]"''', the fictional '''{{ill|Miyamizu Shrine|ja|宮水神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamizu Shrine|qq=}}''' is depicted as enshrining '''Shidori deity Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto (倭文神建葉槌命)'''
== Shizuri Shrines of the Engishiki ==
*'''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Isesaki)|ja|倭文神社 (伊勢崎市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Isesaki)|qq=}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Shidori-go, Nanba District, Kōzuke Province''' – (Higashi-Kaminomiya-cho, [[:en:Isesaki|Isesaki, Gunma]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shizu Shrine|ja|静神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizu Shrine|qq=}} (静神社)''' in '''Kuji District, Hitachi Province''' – ([[:en:Naka, Ibaraki|Naka, Ibaraki]], [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Nirasaki)|ja|倭文神社 (韮崎市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Nirasaki)|qq=}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Koma District, Kai Province''' – (Miyakubo, [[:en:Nirasaki, Yamanashi|Nirasaki, Yamanashi]], [[Yamanashi Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Fujinomiya)|ja|倭文神社 (富士宮市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Fujinomiya)|qq=}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Fuji District, Suruga Province''' – (Hoshiyama, [[:en:Fujinomiya, Shizuoka|Fujinomiya, Shizuoka]], [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]) * '''Shidori Shrine (倭文神社)''' in '''Tagata District, Izu Province''' – Identified with multiple candidate shrines, including '''{{ill|Kuwado Shrine|ja|鍬戸神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuwado Shrine|qq=}}''' ([[:en:Mishima, Shizuoka|Mishima, Shizuoka]], [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]) * '''Shidori Shrine (倭文神社)''' in '''Suzuka District, Ise Province''' – '''{{ill|Kasado Shrine|ja|加佐登神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasado Shrine|qq=}}''' (merged into [[Suzuka, Mie|Suzuka]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Maizuru)|ja|倭文神社 (舞鶴市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Maizuru)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Kasa District, Tango Province''' – (Imada, [[:en:Maizuru|Maizuru, Kyoto]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Yosano)|ja|倭文神社 (与謝野町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Yosano)|qq=}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Yosa District, Tango Province''' – (Mikawachi, [[:en:Yosano, Kyoto|Yosano, Kyoto]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Asago)|ja|倭文神社 (朝来市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Asago)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Asago District, Tajima Province''' – (Maruyama, [[:en:Asago, Hyōgo|Asago, Hyōgo]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Tottori)|ja|倭文神社 (鳥取市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Tottori)|qq=}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Takakusa District, Inaba Province''' – (Shidori, [[:en:Tottori (city)|Tottori, Tottori]], [[Tottori Prefecture]]) * '''[[:en:Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|Shitori Shrine (Tottori)]] (倭文神社)''' in '''Kawamura District, Hōki Province''' – (Miyauchi, [[:en:Yurihama, Tottori|Yurihama, Tottori]], [[Tottori Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Kurayoshi)|ja|倭文神社 (倉吉市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Kurayoshi)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Kume District, Hōki Province''' – (Shizu, [[:en:Kurayoshi, Tottori|Kurayoshi, Tottori]], [[Tottori Prefecture]])
== Other shrines == *'''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Tono)|ja|倭文神社 (遠野市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Tono)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (Tsuchibuchi-cho, [[:en:Tōno, Iwate|Tono, Iwate]], [[:en:Iwate Prefecture|Iwate Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Nara)|ja|倭文神社 (奈良市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Nara)|qq=}}''' (Nishikujo-cho, [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara, Nara]], [[Nara Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Tsuyama)|ja|倭文神社 (津山市油木北)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Tsuyama)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Shidori-go, Kume District, Mimasaka Province''' – (Yukikita, [[:en:Tsuyama|Tsuyama, Okayama]], [[Okayama Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Awaji)|ja|倭文神社 (あわじ市倭文委文)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Awaji)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Shidori-go, Mihara District, Awaji Province''' – (Shidori, [[:en:Minamiawaji|Minamiawaji, Hyōgo]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]])
Additionally, '''{{ill|Ōmika Shrine|8=ja|9=大甕神社|ja|大甕神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmika Shrine|qq=}}''' ([[:en:Hitachi, Ibaraki|Hitachi, Ibaraki]], [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]]) also enshrines '''Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto'''. Similarly, '''{{ill|Ishii Shrine|ja|石井神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishii Shrine|qq=}}''' ([[:en:Kasama, Ibaraki|Kasama, Ibaraki]], [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]]) enshrines the same deity, with a large sacred rock called "Mitarai" located to the south. == See Also ==
* '''{{ill|Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}}''' (also known as Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto) * '''{{ill|Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|ja|天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}''' (also known as Amanoorihime)

== Other shrines == *'''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Tono)|ja|倭文神社 (遠野市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Tono)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (Tsuchibuchi-cho, [[:en:Tōno, Iwate|Tono, Iwate]], [[:en:Iwate Prefecture|Iwate Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Nara)|ja|倭文神社 (奈良市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Nara)|qq=}}''' (Nishikujo-cho, [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Tsuyama)|ja|倭文神社 (津山市油木北)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Tsuyama)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Shidori-go, Kume District, Mimasaka Province''' – (Yukikita, [[:en:Tsuyama|Tsuyama, Okayama]], [[Okayama Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Awaji)|ja|倭文神社 (あわじ市倭文委文)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Awaji)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Shidori-go, Mihara District, Awaji Province''' – (Shidori, [[:en:Minamiawaji|Minamiawaji, Hyōgo]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]])
Additionally, '''{{ill|Ōmika Shrine|8=ja|9=大甕神社|ja|大甕神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmika Shrine|qq=}}''' ([[:en:Hitachi, Ibaraki|Hitachi, Ibaraki]], [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]]) also enshrines '''Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto'''. Similarly, '''{{ill|Ishii Shrine|ja|石井神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishii Shrine|qq=}}''' ([[:en:Kasama, Ibaraki|Kasama, Ibaraki]], [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]]) enshrines the same deity, with a large sacred rock called "Mitarai" located to the south. == See Also ==

revision.text:
'''Shidori Shrine''' (倭文神社) refers to Shinto shrines bearing the name "Shidori" (also read as Shizuri, Shitori, or Shidori).
These shrines enshrine '''{{ill|Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}}''' (also known as '''Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto''', '''Ameno Hachio''', or '''Takehatsuchi''') as the '''deity of weaving''', alongside '''{{ill|Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|ja|天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}''' (also known as '''Amano Yachichihime''' or '''Amanoorihime'''). These shrines were established by the '''{{ill|Shizuri clan|ja|倭文部|1=Shizuri clan|2=ja|3=倭文氏|4=zh|5=倭文氏|6=de|7=Shizuri Clan|8=fr|9=Clan Shizuri|lt=Shizuri Bemin (vassals of the Shizuri clan)|lt=Shizuri clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|qq=}}''', who worshipped Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto as their ancestral deity.
The '''origin of these shrines is attributed to''' the '''{{ill|Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine|ja|葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine|qq=}}''' in '''Katsuragi, Nara Prefecture'''. However, while the introduction and promotion of silk weaving technology in Japan is credited to '''[[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]''', some Shidori Shrines claim their origins date back to the reign of '''[[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]'''. Therefore, these shrines provide '''important historical evidence''' regarding '''when and where silk weaving developed in Japan (Wa).'''
Additionally, in the '''2016 animated film "[[:en:Your_Name|Your Name]]"''', the fictional '''{{ill|Miyamizu Shrine|ja|宮水神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamizu Shrine|qq=}}''' is depicted as enshrining '''Shidori deity Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto (倭文神建葉槌命)'''
== Shizuri Shrines of the Engishiki ==
*'''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Isesaki)|ja|倭文神社 (伊勢崎市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Isesaki)|qq=}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Shidori-go, Nanba District, Kōzuke Province''' – (Higashi-Kaminomiya-cho, [[:en:Isesaki|Isesaki, Gunma]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shizu Shrine|ja|静神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizu Shrine|qq=}} (静神社)''' in '''Kuji District, Hitachi Province''' – ([[:en:Naka, Ibaraki|Naka, Ibaraki]], [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Nirasaki)|ja|倭文神社 * '''Shidori Shrine (倭文神社)''' in '''Suzuka District, Ise Province''' – '''{{ill|Kasado Shrine|ja|加佐登神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasado Shrine|qq=}}''' (merged into [[Suzuka, Mie|Suzuka]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Maizuru)|ja|倭文神社 (舞鶴市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Maizuru)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Asago)|ja|倭文神社 (朝来市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Asago)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Asago District, Tajima Province''' – (Maruyama, [[:en:Asago, Hyōgo|Asago, Hyōgo]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Tottori)|ja|倭文神社 Prefecture]]) * '''[[:en:Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|Shitori Shrine (Tottori)]] (倭文神社)''' in '''Kawamura District, Hōki Province''' – (Miyauchi, [[:en:Yurihama, Tottori|Yurihama, Tottori]], [[Tottori Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Kurayoshi)|ja|倭文神社 (倉吉市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Kurayoshi)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Kume District, Hōki Province''' – (Shizu, [[:en:Kurayoshi, Tottori|Kurayoshi, Tottori]], [[Tottori Prefecture]])
== Other shrines == *'''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Tono)|ja|倭文神社 (遠野市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Tono)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (Tsuchibuchi-cho, [[:en:Tōno, Iwate|Tono, Iwate]], [[:en:Iwate Prefecture|Iwate Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Nara)|ja|倭文神社 (奈良市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Nara)|qq=}}''' (Nishikujo-cho, [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Tsuyama)|ja|倭文神社 (津山市油木北)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Tsuyama)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Shidori-go, Kume District, Mimasaka Province''' – (Yukikita, [[:en:Tsuyama|Tsuyama, Okayama]], [[Okayama Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Awaji)|ja|倭文神社 (あわじ市倭文委文)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Awaji)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Shidori-go, Mihara District, Awaji Province''' – (Shidori, [[:en:Minamiawaji|Minamiawaji, Hyōgo]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]])
Additionally, '''{{ill|Ōmika Shrine|8=ja|9=大甕神社|ja|大甕神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmika Shrine|qq=}}''' ([[:en:Hitachi, Ibaraki|Hitachi, Ibaraki]], [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]]) also enshrines '''Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto'''. Similarly, '''{{ill|Ishii Shrine|ja|石井神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishii Shrine|qq=}}''' ([[:en:Kasama, Ibaraki|Kasama, Ibaraki]], [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]]) enshrines the same deity, with a large sacred rock called "Mitarai" located to the south. == See Also ==

revision.text:
'''Shidori Shrine''' (倭文神社) refers to Shinto shrines bearing the name "Shidori" (also read as Shizuri, Shitori, or Shidori).
These shrines enshrine '''{{ill|Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}}''' (also known as '''Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto''', '''Ameno Hachio''', or '''Takehatsuchi''') as the '''deity of weaving''', alongside '''{{ill|Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|ja|天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}''' (also known as '''Amano Yachichihime''' or '''Amanoorihime'''). These shrines were established by the '''{{ill|Shizuri clan|ja|倭文部|1=Shizuri clan|2=ja|3=倭文氏|4=zh|5=倭文氏|6=de|7=Shizuri Clan|8=fr|9=Clan Shizuri|lt=Shizuri Bemin (vassals of the Shizuri clan)|lt=Shizuri clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|qq=}}''', who worshipped Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto as their ancestral deity.
The '''origin of these shrines is attributed to''' the '''{{ill|Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine|ja|葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine|qq=}}''' in '''Katsuragi, Nara Prefecture'''. However, while the introduction and promotion of silk weaving technology in Japan is credited to '''[[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]''', some Shidori Shrines claim their origins date back to the reign of '''[[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]'''. Therefore, these shrines provide '''important historical evidence''' regarding '''when and where silk weaving developed in Japan (Wa).'''
Additionally, in the '''2016 animated film "[[:en:Your_Name|Your Name]]"''', the fictional '''{{ill|Miyamizu Shrine|ja|宮水神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamizu Shrine|qq=}}''' is depicted as enshrining '''Shidori deity Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto (倭文神建葉槌命)'''
== Shizuri Shrines of the Engishiki ==
*'''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Isesaki)|ja|倭文神社 (伊勢崎市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Isesaki)|qq=}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Shidori-go, Nanba District, Kōzuke Province''' – (Higashi-Kaminomiya-cho, [[:en:Isesaki|Isesaki, Gunma]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shizu Shrine|ja|静神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizu Shrine|qq=}} (静神社)''' in '''Kuji District, Hitachi Province''' – ([[:en:Naka, Ibaraki|Naka, Ibaraki]], [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Nirasaki)|ja|倭文神社 (韮崎市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Nirasaki)|qq=}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Koma District, Kai Province''' – (Miyakubo, [[:en:Nirasaki, Yamanashi|Nirasaki, Yamanashi]], [[Yamanashi Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Fujinomiya)|ja|倭文神社 (富士宮市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Fujinomiya)|qq=}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Fuji District, Suruga Province''' – (Hoshiyama, [[:en:Fujinomiya, Shizuoka|Fujinomiya, Shizuoka]], [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]) * '''Shidori Shrine (倭文神社)''' in '''Tagata District, Izu Province''' – Identified with multiple candidate shrines, including '''{{ill|Kuwado Shrine|ja|鍬戸神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuwado Shrine|qq=}}''' ([[:en:Mishima, Shizuoka|Mishima, Shizuoka]], [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]) * '''Shidori Shrine (倭文神社)''' in '''Suzuka District, Ise Province''' – '''{{ill|Kasado Shrine|ja|加佐登神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasado Shrine|qq=}}''' (merged into [[Suzuka, Mie|Suzuka]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Maizuru)|ja|倭文神社 (舞鶴市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Maizuru)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Kasa District, Tango Province''' – (Imada, [[:en:Maizuru|Maizuru, Kyoto]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Yosano)|ja|倭文神社 (与謝野町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Yosano)|qq=}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Yosa District, Tango Province''' – (Mikawachi, [[:en:Yosano, Kyoto|Yosano, Kyoto]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Asago)|ja|倭文神社 (朝来市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Asago)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Asago District, Tajima Province''' – (Maruyama, [[:en:Asago, Hyōgo|Asago, Hyōgo]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Tottori)|ja|倭文神社 (鳥取市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Tottori)|qq=}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Takakusa District, Inaba Province''' – (Shidori, [[:en:Tottori (city)|Tottori, Tottori]], [[Tottori Prefecture]]) * '''[[:en:Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|Shitori Shrine (Tottori)]] (倭文神社)''' in '''Kawamura District, Hōki Province''' – (Miyauchi, [[:en:Yurihama, Tottori|Yurihama, Tottori]], [[Tottori Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Kurayoshi)|ja|倭文神社 (倉吉市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Kurayoshi)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Kume District, Hōki Province''' – (Shizu, [[:en:Kurayoshi, Tottori|Kurayoshi, Tottori]], [[Tottori Prefecture]])
== Other shrines == *'''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Tono)|ja|倭文神社 (遠野市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Tono)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (Tsuchibuchi-cho, [[:en:Tōno, Iwate|Tono, Iwate]], [[:en:Iwate Prefecture|Iwate Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Nara)|ja|倭文神社 (奈良市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Nara)|qq=}}''' (Nishikujo-cho, [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Tsuyama)|ja|倭文神社 (津山市油木北)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Tsuyama)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Shidori-go, Kume District, Mimasaka Province''' – (Yukikita, [[:en:Tsuyama|Tsuyama, Okayama]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Awaji)|ja|倭文神社 (あわじ市倭文委文)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Awaji)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Shidori-go, Mihara District, Awaji Province''' – (Shidori, [[:en:Minamiawaji|Minamiawaji, Hyōgo]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]])
Additionally, '''{{ill|Ōmika Shrine|8=ja|9=大甕神社|ja|大甕神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmika Shrine|qq=}}''' ([[:en:Hitachi, Ibaraki|Hitachi, Ibaraki]], [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]]) also enshrines '''Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto'''. Similarly, '''{{ill|Ishii Shrine|ja|石井神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishii Shrine|qq=}}''' ([[:en:Kasama, Ibaraki|Kasama, Ibaraki]], [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]]) enshrines the same deity, with a large sacred rock called "Mitarai" located to the south. == See Also ==
* '''{{ill|Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}}''' (also known as Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto) * '''{{ill|Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|ja|天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}''' (also known as Amanoorihime)

'''Shidori Shrine''' (倭文神社) refers to Shinto shrines bearing the name "Shidori" (also read as Shizuri, Shitori, or Shidori).
These shrines enshrine '''{{ill|Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}}''' (also known as '''Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto''', '''Ameno Hachio''', or '''Takehatsuchi''') as the '''deity of weaving''', alongside '''{{ill|Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|ja|天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}''' (also known as '''Amano Yachichihime''' or '''Amanoorihime'''). These shrines were established by the '''{{ill|Shizuri clan|ja|倭文部|1=Shizuri clan|2=ja|3=倭文氏|4=zh|5=倭文氏|6=de|7=Shizuri Clan|8=fr|9=Clan Shizuri|lt=Shizuri Bemin (vassals of the Shizuri clan)|lt=Shizuri clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|qq=}}''', who worshipped Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto as their ancestral deity.
The '''origin of these shrines is attributed to''' the '''{{ill|Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine|ja|葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine|qq=}}''' in '''Katsuragi, Nara Prefecture'''. However, while the introduction and promotion of silk weaving technology in Japan is credited to '''[[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]''', some Shidori Shrines claim their origins date back to the reign of '''[[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]'''. Therefore, these shrines provide '''important historical evidence''' regarding '''when and where silk weaving developed in Japan (Wa).'''
Additionally, in the '''2016 animated film "[[:en:Your_Name|Your Name]]"''', the fictional '''{{ill|Miyamizu Shrine|ja|宮水神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamizu Shrine|qq=}}''' is depicted as enshrining '''Shidori deity Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto (倭文神建葉槌命)'''

District, Hōki Province''' – (Shizu, [[:en:Kurayoshi, Tottori|Kurayoshi, Tottori]], [[Tottori Prefecture]])
== Other shrines == *'''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Tono)|ja|倭文神社 (遠野市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Tono)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (Tsuchibuchi-cho, [[:en:Tōno, Iwate|Tono, Iwate]], [[:en:Iwate Prefecture|Iwate Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Nara)|ja|倭文神社 (奈良市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Nara)|qq=}}''' (Nishikujo-cho, [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Tsuyama)|ja|倭文神社 (津山市油木北)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Tsuyama)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Shidori-go, Kume District, Mimasaka Province''' – (Yukikita, [[:en:Tsuyama|Tsuyama, Okayama]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Awaji)|ja|倭文神社 (あわじ市倭文委文)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Awaji)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Shidori-go, Mihara District, Awaji Province''' – (Shidori, [[:en:Minamiawaji|Minamiawaji, Hyōgo]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]])
Additionally, '''{{ill|Ōmika Shrine|8=ja|9=大甕神社|ja|大甕神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmika Shrine|qq=}}''' ([[:en:Hitachi, Ibaraki|Hitachi, Ibaraki]], [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]]) also enshrines '''Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto'''. Similarly, '''{{ill|Ishii Shrine|ja|石井神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishii Shrine|qq=}}''' ([[:en:Kasama, Ibaraki|Kasama, Ibaraki]], [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]]) enshrines the same deity, with a large sacred rock called "Mitarai" located to the south. == See Also ==
* '''{{ill|Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}}''' (also known as Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto) * '''{{ill|Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|ja|天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}''' (also known as Amanoorihime)
