revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:倭文神社]][[zh:倭文神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:倭文神社]][[zh:倭文神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:倭文神社]][[zh:倭文神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11387088]] Wikidata name: shitori shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:28, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Shidori Shrine''' (倭文神社) refers to Shinto shrines bearing the name "Shidori" (also read as Shizuri, Shitori, or Shidori).
These shrines enshrine '''{{ill|Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}}''' (also known as '''Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto''', '''Ameno Hachio''', or '''Takehatsuchi''') as the '''deity of weaving''', alongside '''{{ill|Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|ja|天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}''' (also known as '''Amano Yachichihime''' or '''Amanoorihime'''). These shrines were established by the '''{{ill|Shizuri clan|ja|倭文部|1=Shizuri clan|2=ja|3=倭文氏|4=zh|5=倭文氏|6=de|7=Shizuri Clan|8=fr|9=Clan Shizuri|lt=Shizuri Bemin (vassals of the Shizuri clan)|lt=Shizuri clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|qq=}}''', who worshipped Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto as their ancestral deity.
The '''origin of these shrines is attributed to''' the '''{{ill|Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine|ja|葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine|qq=}}''' in '''Katsuragi, Nara Prefecture'''. However, while the introduction and promotion of silk weaving technology in Japan is credited to '''[[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]''', some Shidori Shrines claim their origins date back to the reign of '''[[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]'''. Therefore, these shrines provide '''important historical evidence''' regarding '''when and where silk weaving developed in Japan (Wa).'''
Additionally, in the '''2016 animated film "[[:en:Your_Name|Your Name]]"''', the fictional '''{{ill|Miyamizu Shrine|ja|宮水神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamizu Shrine|1=Miyamizu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q38276233}}''' is depicted as enshrining '''Shidori deity Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto (倭文神建葉槌命)'''
== Shizuri Shrines of the Engishiki ==
*'''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Isesaki)|ja|倭文神社 (伊勢崎市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Isesaki)|qq=}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Shidori-go, Nanba District, Kōzuke Province''' – (Higashi-Kaminomiya-cho, [[:en:Isesaki|Isesaki, Gunma]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shizu Shrine|ja|静神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizu Shrine|qq=}} (静神社)''' in '''Kuji District, Hitachi Province''' – ([[:en:Naka, Ibaraki|Naka, Ibaraki]], [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Nirasaki)|ja|倭文神社 (韮崎市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Nirasaki)|qq=}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Koma District, Kai Province''' – (Miyakubo, [[:en:Nirasaki, Yamanashi|Nirasaki, Yamanashi]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Fujinomiya)|ja|倭文神社 (富士宮市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Fujinomiya)|qq=}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Fuji District, Suruga Province''' – (Hoshiyama, [[:en:Fujinomiya, Shizuoka|Fujinomiya, Shizuoka]], [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]]) * '''Shidori Shrine (倭文神社)''' in '''Tagata District, Izu Province''' – Identified with multiple candidate shrines, including '''{{ill|Kuwado Shrine|ja|鍬戸神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuwado Shrine|qq=}}''' ([[:en:Mishima, Shizuoka|Mishima, Shizuoka]], [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]]) * '''Shidori Shrine (倭文神社)''' in '''Suzuka District, Ise Province''' – '''{{ill|Kasado Shrine|ja|加佐登神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasado Shrine|qq=}}''' (merged into [[:en:Suzuka, Mie|Suzuka]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Maizuru)|ja|倭文神社 (舞鶴市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Maizuru)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Kasa District, Tango Province''' – (Imada, [[:en:Maizuru|Maizuru, Kyoto]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Yosano)|ja|倭文神社 (与謝野町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Yosano)|qq=}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Yosa District, Tango Province''' – (Mikawachi, [[:en:Yosano, Kyoto|Yosano, Kyoto]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Asago)|ja|倭文神社 (朝来市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Asago)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Asago District, Tajima Province''' – (Maruyama, [[:en:Asago, Hyōgo|Asago, Hyōgo]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Tottori)|ja|倭文神社 (鳥取市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Tottori)|qq=}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Takakusa District, Inaba Province''' – (Shidori, [[:en:Tottori (city)|Tottori, Tottori]], [[:en:Tottori Prefecture|Tottori Prefecture]]) * '''[[:en:Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|Shitori Shrine (Tottori)]] (倭文神社)''' in '''Kawamura District, Hōki Province''' – (Miyauchi, [[:en:Yurihama, Tottori|Yurihama, Tottori]], [[:en:Tottori Prefecture|Tottori Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Kurayoshi)|ja|倭文神社 (倉吉市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Kurayoshi)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Kume District, Hōki Province''' – (Shizu, [[:en:Kurayoshi, Tottori|Kurayoshi, Tottori]], [[:en:Tottori Prefecture|Tottori Prefecture]])
== Other shrines == *'''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Tono)|ja|倭文神社 (遠野市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Tono)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (Tsuchibuchi-cho, [[:en:Tōno, Iwate|Tono, Iwate]], [[:en:Iwate Prefecture|Iwate Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Nara)|ja|倭文神社 (奈良市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Nara)|qq=}}''' (Nishikujo-cho, [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Tsuyama)|ja|倭文神社 (津山市油木北)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Tsuyama)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Shidori-go, Kume District, Mimasaka Province''' – (Yukikita, [[:en:Tsuyama|Tsuyama, Okayama]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Awaji)|ja|倭文神社 (あわじ市倭文委文)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Awaji)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Shidori-go, Mihara District, Awaji Province''' – (Shidori, [[:en:Minamiawaji|Minamiawaji, Hyōgo]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]])
Additionally, '''{{ill|Ōmika Shrine|8=ja|9=大甕神社|ja|大甕神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmika Shrine|qq=}}''' ([[:en:Hitachi, Ibaraki|Hitachi, Ibaraki]], [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]]) also enshrines '''Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto'''. Similarly, '''{{ill|Ishii Shrine|ja|石井神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishii Shrine|qq=}}''' ([[:en:Kasama, Ibaraki|Kasama, Ibaraki]], [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]]) enshrines the same deity, with a large sacred rock called "Mitarai" located to the south. == See Also ==
* '''{{ill|Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}}''' (also known as Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto) * '''{{ill|Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|ja|天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}''' (also known as Amanoorihime)

These shrines were established by the '''{{ill|Shizuri clan|ja|倭文部|1=Shizuri clan|2=ja|3=倭文氏|4=zh|5=倭文氏|6=de|7=Shizuri Clan|8=fr|9=Clan Shizuri|lt=Shizuri Bemin (vassals of the Shizuri clan)|lt=Shizuri clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|qq=}}''', who worshipped Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto as their ancestral deity.

Therefore, these shrines provide '''important historical evidence''' regarding '''when and where silk weaving developed in Japan (Wa).'''

(倉吉市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Kurayoshi)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Kume District, Hōki Province''' – (Shizu, [[:en:Kurayoshi, Tottori|Kurayoshi, Tottori]], [[:en:Tottori Prefecture|Tottori Prefecture]])
== Other shrines == *'''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Tono)|ja|倭文神社 (遠野市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Tono)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (Tsuchibuchi-cho, [[:en:Tōno, Iwate|Tono, Iwate]], [[:en:Iwate Prefecture|Iwate Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Nara)|ja|倭文神社 (奈良市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Nara)|qq=}}''' (Nishikujo-cho, [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Tsuyama)|ja|倭文神社 (津山市油木北)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Tsuyama)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Shidori-go, Kume District, Mimasaka Province''' – (Yukikita, [[:en:Tsuyama|Tsuyama, Okayama]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Awaji)|ja|倭文神社 (あわじ市倭文委文)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Awaji)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Shidori-go, Mihara District, Awaji Province''' – (Shidori, [[:en:Minamiawaji|Minamiawaji, Hyōgo]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]])
Additionally, '''{{ill|Ōmika Shrine|8=ja|9=大甕神社|ja|大甕神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmika Shrine|qq=}}''' ([[:en:Hitachi, Ibaraki|Hitachi, Ibaraki]], [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]]) also enshrines '''Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto'''. Similarly, '''{{ill|Ishii Shrine|ja|石井神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishii Shrine|qq=}}''' ([[:en:Kasama, Ibaraki|Kasama, Ibaraki]], [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]]) enshrines the same deity, with a large sacred rock called "Mitarai" located to the south. == See Also ==
* '''{{ill|Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}}''' (also known as Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto) * '''{{ill|Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|ja|天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}''' (also known as Amanoorihime)

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:倭文神社]][[zh:倭文神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:倭文神社]][[zh:倭文神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:倭文神社]][[zh:倭文神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11387088]] Wikidata name: shitori shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:28, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Shidori Shrine''' (倭文神社) refers to Shinto shrines bearing the name "Shidori" (also read as Shizuri, Shitori, or Shidori).
These shrines enshrine '''{{ill|Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}}''' (also known as '''Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto''', '''Ameno Hachio''', or '''Takehatsuchi''') as the '''deity of weaving''', alongside '''{{ill|Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|ja|天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}''' (also known as '''Amano Yachichihime''' or '''Amanoorihime'''). These shrines were established by the '''{{ill|Shizuri clan|ja|倭文部|1=Shizuri clan|2=ja|3=倭文氏|4=zh|5=倭文氏|6=de|7=Shizuri Clan|8=fr|9=Clan Shizuri|lt=Shizuri Bemin (vassals of the Shizuri clan)|lt=Shizuri clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|qq=}}''', who worshipped Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto as their ancestral deity.
The '''origin of these shrines is attributed to''' the '''{{ill|Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine|ja|葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine|qq=}}''' in '''Katsuragi, Nara Prefecture'''. However, while the introduction and promotion of silk weaving technology in Japan is credited to '''[[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]''', some Shidori Shrines claim their origins date back to the reign of '''[[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]'''. Therefore, these shrines provide '''important historical evidence''' regarding '''when and where silk weaving developed in Japan (Wa).'''
Additionally, in the '''2016 animated film "[[:en:Your_Name|Your Name]]"''', the fictional '''{{ill|Miyamizu Shrine|ja|宮水神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamizu Shrine|1=Miyamizu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q38276233}}''' is depicted as enshrining '''Shidori deity Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto (倭文神建葉槌命)'''
== Shizuri Shrines of the Engishiki ==
*'''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Isesaki)|ja|倭文神社 (伊勢崎市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Isesaki)|qq=}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Shidori-go, Nanba District, Kōzuke Province''' – (Higashi-Kaminomiya-cho, [[:en:Isesaki|Isesaki, Gunma]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shizu Shrine|ja|静神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizu Shrine|qq=}} (静神社)''' in '''Kuji District, Hitachi Province''' – ([[:en:Naka, Ibaraki|Naka, Ibaraki]], [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Nirasaki)|ja|倭文神社 (韮崎市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Nirasaki)|qq=}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Koma District, Kai Province''' – (Miyakubo, [[:en:Nirasaki, Yamanashi|Nirasaki, Yamanashi]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Fujinomiya)|ja|倭文神社 (富士宮市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Fujinomiya)|qq=}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Fuji District, Suruga Province''' – (Hoshiyama, [[:en:Fujinomiya, Shizuoka|Fujinomiya, Shizuoka]], [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]]) * '''Shidori Shrine (倭文神社)''' in '''Tagata District, Izu Province''' – Identified with multiple candidate shrines, including '''{{ill|Kuwado Shrine|ja|鍬戸神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuwado Shrine|qq=}}''' ([[:en:Mishima, Shizuoka|Mishima, Shizuoka]], [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]]) * '''Shidori Shrine (倭文神社)''' in '''Suzuka District, Ise Province''' – '''{{ill|Kasado Shrine|ja|加佐登神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasado Shrine|qq=}}''' (merged into [[:en:Suzuka, Mie|Suzuka]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Maizuru)|ja|倭文神社 (舞鶴市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Maizuru)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Kasa District, Tango Province''' – (Imada, [[:en:Maizuru|Maizuru, Kyoto]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Yosano)|ja|倭文神社 (与謝野町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Yosano)|qq=}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Yosa District, Tango Province''' – (Mikawachi, [[:en:Yosano, Kyoto|Yosano, Kyoto]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Asago)|ja|倭文神社 (朝来市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Asago)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Asago District, Tajima Province''' – (Maruyama, [[:en:Asago, Hyōgo|Asago, Hyōgo]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Tottori)|ja|倭文神社 (鳥取市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Tottori)|qq=}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Takakusa District, Inaba Province''' – (Shidori, [[:en:Tottori (city)|Tottori, Tottori]], [[:en:Tottori Prefecture|Tottori Prefecture]]) * '''[[:en:Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|Shitori Shrine (Tottori)]] (倭文神社)''' in '''Kawamura District, Hōki Province''' – (Miyauchi, [[:en:Yurihama, Tottori|Yurihama, Tottori]], [[:en:Tottori Prefecture|Tottori Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Kurayoshi)|ja|倭文神社 (倉吉市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Kurayoshi)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Kume District, Hōki Province''' – (Shizu, [[:en:Kurayoshi, Tottori|Kurayoshi, Tottori]], [[:en:Tottori Prefecture|Tottori Prefecture]])
== Other shrines == *'''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Tono)|ja|倭文神社 (遠野市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Tono)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (Tsuchibuchi-cho, [[:en:Tōno, Iwate|Tono, Iwate]], [[:en:Iwate Prefecture|Iwate Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Nara)|ja|倭文神社 (奈良市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Nara)|qq=}}''' (Nishikujo-cho, [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Tsuyama)|ja|倭文神社 (津山市油木北)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Tsuyama)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Shidori-go, Kume District, Mimasaka Province''' – (Yukikita, [[:en:Tsuyama|Tsuyama, Okayama]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Awaji)|ja|倭文神社 (あわじ市倭文委文)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Awaji)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Shidori-go, Mihara District, Awaji Province''' – (Shidori, [[:en:Minamiawaji|Minamiawaji, Hyōgo]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]])
Additionally, '''{{ill|Ōmika Shrine|8=ja|9=大甕神社|ja|大甕神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmika Shrine|qq=}}''' ([[:en:Hitachi, Ibaraki|Hitachi, Ibaraki]], [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]]) also enshrines '''Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto'''. Similarly, '''{{ill|Ishii Shrine|ja|石井神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishii Shrine|qq=}}''' ([[:en:Kasama, Ibaraki|Kasama, Ibaraki]], [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]]) enshrines the same deity, with a large sacred rock called "Mitarai" located to the south. == See Also ==
* '''{{ill|Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}}''' (also known as Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto) * '''{{ill|Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|ja|天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}''' (also known as Amanoorihime)

These shrines were established by the '''{{ill|Shizuri clan|ja|倭文部|1=Shizuri clan|2=ja|3=倭文氏|4=zh|5=倭文氏|6=de|7=Shizuri Clan|8=fr|9=Clan Shizuri|lt=Shizuri Bemin (vassals of the Shizuri clan)|lt=Shizuri clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|qq=}}''', who worshipped Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto as their ancestral deity.
The '''origin of these shrines is attributed to''' the '''{{ill|Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine|ja|葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine|qq=}}''' in '''Katsuragi, Nara Prefecture'''. However, while the introduction and promotion of silk weaving technology in Japan is credited to '''[[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]''', some Shidori Shrines claim their origins date back to the reign of '''[[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]'''. Therefore, these shrines provide '''important historical evidence''' regarding '''when and where silk weaving developed in Japan (Wa).'''
Additionally, in the '''2016 animated film "[[:en:Your_Name|Your Name]]"''', the fictional '''{{ill|Miyamizu Shrine|ja|宮水神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamizu Shrine|1=Miyamizu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q38276233}}''' is depicted as enshrining '''Shidori deity Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto (倭文神建葉槌命)'''
== Shizuri Shrines of the Engishiki ==
*'''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Isesaki)|ja|倭文神社 (伊勢崎市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Isesaki)|qq=}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Shidori-go, Nanba District, Kōzuke Province''' – (Higashi-Kaminomiya-cho, [[:en:Isesaki|Isesaki, Gunma]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shizu Shrine|ja|静神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizu Shrine|qq=}} (静神社)''' in '''Kuji District, Hitachi [[:en:Tottori Prefecture|Tottori Prefecture]])
== Other shrines == *'''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Tono)|ja|倭文神社 (遠野市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Tono)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (Tsuchibuchi-cho, [[:en:Tōno, Iwate|Tono, Iwate]], [[:en:Iwate Prefecture|Iwate Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Nara)|ja|倭文神社 (奈良市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Nara)|qq=}}''' (Nishikujo-cho, [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Tsuyama)|ja|倭文神社 (津山市油木北)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Tsuyama)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Shidori-go, Kume District, Mimasaka Province''' – (Yukikita, [[:en:Tsuyama|Tsuyama, Okayama]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Awaji)|ja|倭文神社 (あわじ市倭文委文)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Awaji)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Shidori-go, Mihara District, Awaji Province''' – (Shidori, [[:en:Minamiawaji|Minamiawaji, Hyōgo]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]])
Additionally, '''{{ill|Ōmika Shrine|8=ja|9=大甕神社|ja|大甕神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmika Shrine|qq=}}''' ([[:en:Hitachi, Ibaraki|Hitachi, Ibaraki]], [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]]) also enshrines '''Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto'''. Similarly, '''{{ill|Ishii Shrine|ja|石井神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishii Shrine|qq=}}''' ([[:en:Kasama, Ibaraki|Kasama, Ibaraki]], [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]]) enshrines the same deity, with a large sacred rock called "Mitarai" located to the south. == See Also ==
* '''{{ill|Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}}''' (also known as Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto) * '''{{ill|Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|ja|天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}''' (also known as Amanoorihime)

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:倭文神社]][[zh:倭文神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:倭文神社]][[zh:倭文神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:倭文神社]][[zh:倭文神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11387088]] Wikidata name: shitori shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:28, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Shidori Shrine''' (倭文神社) refers to Shinto shrines bearing the name "Shidori" (also read as Shizuri, Shitori, or Shidori).
These shrines enshrine '''{{ill|Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}}''' (also known as '''Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto''', '''Ameno Hachio''', or '''Takehatsuchi''') as the '''deity of weaving''', alongside '''{{ill|Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|ja|天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}''' (also known as '''Amano Yachichihime''' or '''Amanoorihime'''). These shrines were established by the '''{{ill|Shizuri clan|ja|倭文部|1=Shizuri clan|2=ja|3=倭文氏|4=zh|5=倭文氏|6=de|7=Shizuri Clan|8=fr|9=Clan Shizuri|lt=Shizuri Bemin (vassals of the Shizuri clan)|lt=Shizuri clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|qq=}}''', who worshipped Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto as their ancestral deity.
The '''origin of these shrines is attributed to''' the '''{{ill|Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine|ja|葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine|qq=}}''' in '''Katsuragi, Nara Prefecture'''. However, while the introduction and promotion of silk weaving technology in Japan is credited to '''[[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]''', some Shidori Shrines claim their origins date back to the reign of '''[[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]'''. Therefore, these shrines provide '''important historical evidence''' regarding '''when and where silk weaving developed in Japan (Wa).'''
Additionally, in the '''2016 animated film "[[:en:Your_Name|Your Name]]"''', the fictional '''{{ill|Miyamizu Shrine|ja|宮水神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamizu Shrine|1=Miyamizu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q38276233}}''' is depicted as enshrining '''Shidori deity Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto (倭文神建葉槌命)'''
== Shizuri Shrines of the Engishiki ==
*'''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Isesaki)|ja|倭文神社 (伊勢崎市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Isesaki)|qq=}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Shidori-go, Nanba District, Kōzuke Province''' – (Higashi-Kaminomiya-cho, [[:en:Isesaki|Isesaki, Gunma]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shizu Shrine|ja|静神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizu Shrine|qq=}} (静神社)''' in '''Kuji District, Hitachi Province''' – ([[:en:Naka, Ibaraki|Naka, Ibaraki]], [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Nirasaki)|ja|倭文神社 (韮崎市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine Shizuoka]], [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]]) * '''Shidori Shrine (倭文神社)''' in '''Tagata District, Izu Province''' – Identified with multiple candidate shrines, including (倭文神社)''' in '''Suzuka District, Ise Province''' – '''{{ill|Kasado Shrine|ja|加佐登神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasado Shrine|qq=}}''' (merged into [[:en:Suzuka, Mie|Suzuka]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Maizuru)|ja|倭文神社 (舞鶴市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Maizuru)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Kasa District, Tango Province''' – (Imada, [[:en:Maizuru|Maizuru, Kyoto]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Yosano)|ja|倭文神社 (与謝野町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Yosano)|qq=}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Yosa District, Tango Province''' – (Mikawachi, [[:en:Yosano, Kyoto|Yosano, Kyoto]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Asago)|ja|倭文神社 (朝来市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Asago)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Asago District, Tajima Province''' – (Maruyama, [[:en:Asago, Hyōgo|Asago, Hyōgo]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Tottori)|ja|倭文神社 (鳥取市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Tottori)|qq=}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Takakusa District, Inaba Province''' – (Shidori, [[:en:Tottori (city)|Tottori, Tottori]], [[:en:Tottori Prefecture|Tottori Prefecture]]) * '''[[:en:Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|Shitori Shrine (Tottori)]] (倭文神社)''' in '''Kawamura District, Hōki Province''' – (Miyauchi, [[:en:Yurihama, Tottori|Yurihama, Tottori]], [[:en:Tottori Prefecture|Tottori Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Kurayoshi)|ja|倭文神社 (倉吉市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Kurayoshi)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Kume District, Hōki Province''' – (Shizu, [[:en:Kurayoshi, Tottori|Kurayoshi, Tottori]], [[:en:Tottori Prefecture|Tottori Prefecture]])
== Other shrines == *'''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Tono)|ja|倭文神社 (遠野市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Tono)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (Tsuchibuchi-cho, [[:en:Tōno, Iwate|Tono, Iwate]], [[:en:Iwate Prefecture|Iwate Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Nara)|ja|倭文神社 (奈良市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Nara)|qq=}}''' (Nishikujo-cho, [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Tsuyama)|ja|倭文神社 (津山市油木北)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Tsuyama)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Shidori-go, Kume District, Mimasaka Province''' – (Yukikita, [[:en:Tsuyama|Tsuyama, Okayama]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Awaji)|ja|倭文神社 (あわじ市倭文委文)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Awaji)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Shidori-go, Mihara District, Awaji Province''' – (Shidori, [[:en:Minamiawaji|Minamiawaji, Hyōgo]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]])
Additionally, '''{{ill|Ōmika Shrine|8=ja|9=大甕神社|ja|大甕神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmika Shrine|qq=}}''' ([[:en:Hitachi, Ibaraki|Hitachi, Ibaraki]], [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]]) also enshrines '''Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto'''. Similarly, '''{{ill|Ishii Shrine|ja|石井神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishii Shrine|qq=}}''' ([[:en:Kasama, Ibaraki|Kasama, Ibaraki]], [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]]) enshrines the same deity, with a large sacred rock called "Mitarai" located to the south. == See Also ==
* '''{{ill|Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}}''' (also known as Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto) * '''{{ill|Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|ja|天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}''' (also known as Amanoorihime)

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Qq]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:倭文神社]][[zh:倭文神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:倭文神社]][[zh:倭文神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:倭文神社]][[zh:倭文神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11387088]] Wikidata name: shitori shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:28, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Shidori Shrine''' (倭文神社) refers to Shinto shrines bearing the name "Shidori" (also read as Shizuri, Shitori, or Shidori).
These shrines enshrine '''{{ill|Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}}''' (also known as '''Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto''', '''Ameno Hachio''', or '''Takehatsuchi''') as the '''deity of weaving''', alongside '''{{ill|Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|ja|天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}''' (also known as '''Amano Yachichihime''' or '''Amanoorihime'''). These shrines were established by the '''{{ill|Shizuri clan|ja|倭文部|1=Shizuri clan|2=ja|3=倭文氏|4=zh|5=倭文氏|6=de|7=Shizuri Clan|8=fr|9=Clan Shizuri|lt=Shizuri Bemin (vassals of the Shizuri clan)|lt=Shizuri clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|qq=}}''', who worshipped Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto as their ancestral deity.
The '''origin of these shrines is attributed to''' the '''{{ill|Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine|ja|葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine|qq=}}''' in '''Katsuragi, Nara Prefecture'''. However, while the introduction and promotion of silk weaving technology in Japan is credited to '''[[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]''', some Shidori Shrines claim their origins date back to the reign of '''[[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]'''. Therefore, these shrines provide '''important historical evidence''' regarding '''when and where silk weaving developed in Japan (Wa).'''
Additionally, in the '''2016 animated film "[[:en:Your_Name|Your Name]]"''', the fictional '''{{ill|Miyamizu Shrine|ja|宮水神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamizu Shrine|1=Miyamizu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q38276233}}''' is depicted as enshrining '''Shidori deity Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto (倭文神建葉槌命)'''
== Shizuri Shrines of the Engishiki ==
*'''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Isesaki)|ja|倭文神社 (伊勢崎市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Isesaki)|qq=}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Shidori-go, Nanba District, Kōzuke Province''' – (Higashi-Kaminomiya-cho, [[:en:Isesaki|Isesaki, Gunma]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shizu Shrine|ja|静神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizu Shrine|qq=}} (静神社)''' in '''Kuji District, Hitachi Province''' – ([[:en:Naka, Ibaraki|Naka, Ibaraki]], [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Nirasaki)|ja|倭文神社 (韮崎市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Nirasaki)|qq=}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Koma District, Kai Province''' – (Miyakubo, [[:en:Nirasaki, Yamanashi|Nirasaki, Yamanashi]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Fujinomiya)|ja|倭文神社 (富士宮市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Fujinomiya)|qq=}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Fuji District, Suruga Province''' – (Hoshiyama, [[:en:Fujinomiya, Shizuoka|Fujinomiya, Shizuoka]], [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]]) * '''Shidori Shrine (倭文神社)''' in '''Tagata District, Izu Province''' – Identified with multiple candidate shrines, including '''{{ill|Kuwado Shrine|ja|鍬戸神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuwado Shrine|qq=}}''' ([[:en:Mishima, Shizuoka|Mishima, Shizuoka]], [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]]) * '''Shidori Shrine (倭文神社)''' in '''Suzuka District, Ise Province''' – '''{{ill|Kasado Shrine|ja|加佐登神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasado Shrine|qq=}}''' (merged into [[:en:Suzuka, Mie|Suzuka]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Maizuru)|ja|倭文神社 (舞鶴市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Maizuru)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Kasa District, Tango Province''' – (Imada, [[:en:Maizuru|Maizuru, Kyoto]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Yosano)|ja|倭文神社 (与謝野町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Yosano)|qq=}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Yosa District, Tango Province''' – (Mikawachi, [[:en:Yosano, Kyoto|Yosano, Kyoto]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Asago)|ja|倭文神社 (朝来市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Asago)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Asago District, Tajima Province''' – (Maruyama, [[:en:Asago, Hyōgo|Asago, Hyōgo]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Tottori)|ja|倭文神社 (鳥取市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Tottori)|qq=}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Takakusa District, Inaba Province''' – (Shidori, [[:en:Tottori (city)|Tottori, Tottori]], [[:en:Tottori Prefecture|Tottori Prefecture]]) * '''[[:en:Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|Shitori Shrine (Tottori)]] (倭文神社)''' in '''Kawamura District, Hōki Province''' – (Miyauchi, [[:en:Yurihama, Tottori|Yurihama, Tottori]], [[:en:Tottori Prefecture|Tottori Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Kurayoshi)|ja|倭文神社 (倉吉市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Kurayoshi)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Kume District, Hōki Province''' – (Shizu, [[:en:Kurayoshi, Tottori|Kurayoshi, Tottori]], [[:en:Tottori Prefecture|Tottori Prefecture]])
== Other shrines == *'''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Tono)|ja|倭文神社 (遠野市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Tono)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (Tsuchibuchi-cho, [[:en:Tōno, Iwate|Tono, Iwate]], [[:en:Iwate Prefecture|Iwate Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Nara)|ja|倭文神社 (奈良市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Nara)|qq=}}''' (Nishikujo-cho, [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Tsuyama)|ja|倭文神社 (津山市油木北)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Tsuyama)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Shidori-go, Kume District, Mimasaka Province''' – (Yukikita, [[:en:Tsuyama|Tsuyama, Okayama]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Awaji)|ja|倭文神社 (あわじ市倭文委文)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Awaji)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Shidori-go, Mihara District, Awaji Province''' – (Shidori, [[:en:Minamiawaji|Minamiawaji, Hyōgo]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]])
Additionally, '''{{ill|Ōmika Shrine|8=ja|9=大甕神社|ja|大甕神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmika Shrine|qq=}}''' ([[:en:Hitachi, Ibaraki|Hitachi, Ibaraki]], [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]]) also enshrines '''Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto'''. Similarly, '''{{ill|Ishii Shrine|ja|石井神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishii Shrine|qq=}}''' ([[:en:Kasama, Ibaraki|Kasama, Ibaraki]], [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]]) enshrines the same deity, with a large sacred rock called "Mitarai" located to the south. == See Also ==
* '''{{ill|Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}}''' (also known as Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto) * '''{{ill|Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|ja|天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}''' (also known as Amanoorihime)

These shrines enshrine '''{{ill|Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}}''' (also known as '''Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto''', '''Ameno Hachio''', or '''Takehatsuchi''') as the '''deity of weaving''', alongside '''{{ill|Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|ja|天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}''' (also known as '''Amano Yachichihime''' or '''Amanoorihime'''). These shrines were established by the '''{{ill|Shizuri clan|ja|倭文部|1=Shizuri clan|2=ja|3=倭文氏|4=zh|5=倭文氏|6=de|7=Shizuri Clan|8=fr|9=Clan Shizuri|lt=Shizuri Bemin (vassals of the Shizuri clan)|lt=Shizuri clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|qq=}}''', who worshipped Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto as their ancestral deity.
The '''origin of these shrines is attributed to''' the '''{{ill|Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine|ja|葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine|qq=}}''' in '''Katsuragi, Nara Prefecture'''. However, while the introduction and promotion of silk weaving technology in Japan is credited to '''{{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|WD=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku}}''', some Shidori Shrines claim their origins date back to the reign of '''{{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|WD=Q333530|lt=Emperor Sujin}}'''. Therefore, these shrines provide '''important historical evidence''' regarding '''when and where silk weaving developed in Japan (Wa).'''
Additionally, in the '''2016 animated film "{{ill|Your_Name|en|Your_Name|WD=Q21697406|lt=Your Name}}"''', the fictional '''{{ill|Miyamizu Shrine|ja|宮水神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamizu Shrine|1=Miyamizu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q38276233}}''' is depicted as enshrining '''Shidori deity Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto (倭文神建葉槌命)'''
== Shizuri Shrines of the Engishiki ==
*'''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Isesaki)|ja|倭文神社 (伊勢崎市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Isesaki)|qq=}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Shidori-go, Nanba District, Kōzuke Province''' – (Higashi-Kaminomiya-cho, {{ill|Isesaki|en|Isesaki|WD=Q328596|lt=Isesaki, Gunma}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|en|Gunma Prefecture|WD=Q129499|lt=Gunma Prefecture}}) * '''{{ill|Shizu Shrine|ja|静神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizu District, Inaba Province''' – (Shidori, {{ill|Tottori (city)|en|Tottori (city)|WD=Q200731|lt=Tottori, Tottori}}, {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|en|Tottori Prefecture|WD=Q133935|lt=Tottori Prefecture}}) * '''{{ill|Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|en|Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|WD=Q245734|lt=Shitori Shrine (Tottori)}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Kawamura District, Hōki Province''' – (Miyauchi, {{ill|Yurihama, Tottori|en|Yurihama, Tottori|WD=Q1156158|lt=Yurihama, Tottori}}, {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|en|Tottori Prefecture|WD=Q133935|lt=Tottori Prefecture}}) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Kurayoshi)|ja|倭文神社 (倉吉市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Kurayoshi)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Kume District, Hōki Province''' – (Shizu, {{ill|Kurayoshi, Tottori|en|Kurayoshi, Tottori|WD=Q642006|lt=Kurayoshi, Tottori}}, {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|en|Tottori Prefecture|WD=Q133935|lt=Tottori Prefecture}})
== Other shrines == *'''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Tono)|ja|倭文神社 (遠野市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Tono)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (Tsuchibuchi-cho, {{ill|Tōno, Iwate|en|Tōno, Iwate|WD=Q646766|lt=Tono, Iwate}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|en|Iwate Prefecture|WD=Q48326|lt=Iwate Prefecture}}) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Nara)|ja|倭文神社 (奈良市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Nara)|qq=}}''' (Nishikujo-cho, {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|WD=Q169134|lt=Nara, Nara}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}}) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Tsuyama)|ja|倭文神社 (津山市油木北)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Tsuyama)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Shidori-go, Kume District, Mimasaka Province''' – (Yukikita, {{ill|Tsuyama|en|Tsuyama|WD=Q841125|lt=Tsuyama, Okayama}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|WD=Q132936|lt=Okayama Prefecture}}) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Awaji)|ja|倭文神社 (あわじ市倭文委文)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Awaji)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Shidori-go, Mihara District, Awaji Province''' – (Shidori, {{ill|Minamiawaji|en|Minamiawaji|WD=Q869004|lt=Minamiawaji, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|WD=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture}})
Additionally, '''{{ill|Ōmika Shrine|8=ja|9=大甕神社|ja|大甕神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmika Shrine|qq=}}''' ({{ill|Hitachi, Ibaraki|en|Hitachi, Ibaraki|WD=Q633922|lt=Hitachi, Ibaraki}}, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|en|Ibaraki Prefecture|WD=Q83273|lt=Ibaraki Prefecture}}) also enshrines '''Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto'''. Similarly, '''{{ill|Ishii Shrine|ja|石井神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishii Shrine|qq=}}''' ({{ill|Kasama, Ibaraki|en|Kasama, Ibaraki|WD=Q845851|lt=Kasama, Ibaraki}}, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|en|Ibaraki Prefecture|WD=Q83273|lt=Ibaraki Prefecture}}) enshrines the same deity, with a large sacred rock called "Mitarai" located to the south. == See Also ==
* '''{{ill|Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}}''' (also known as Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto) * '''{{ill|Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|ja|天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}''' (also known as Amanoorihime)

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:倭文神社]][[zh:倭文神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:倭文神社]][[zh:倭文神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:倭文神社]][[zh:倭文神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11387088]] Wikidata name: shitori shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:28, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Shidori Shrine''' (倭文神社) refers to Shinto shrines bearing the name "Shidori" (also read as Shizuri, Shitori, or Shidori).
These shrines enshrine '''{{ill|Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}}''' (also known as '''Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto''', '''Ameno Hachio''', or '''Takehatsuchi''') as the '''deity of weaving''', alongside '''{{ill|Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|ja|天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}''' (also known as '''Amano Yachichihime''' or '''Amanoorihime'''). These shrines were established by the '''{{ill|Shizuri clan|ja|倭文部|1=Shizuri clan|2=ja|3=倭文氏|4=zh|5=倭文氏|6=de|7=Shizuri Clan|8=fr|9=Clan Shizuri|lt=Shizuri Bemin (vassals of the Shizuri clan)|lt=Shizuri clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|qq=}}''', who worshipped Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto as their ancestral deity.
The '''origin of these shrines is attributed to''' the '''{{ill|Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine|ja|葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine|qq=}}''' in '''Katsuragi, Nara Prefecture'''. However, while the introduction and promotion of silk weaving technology in Japan is credited to '''{{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|WD=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku}}''', some Shidori Shrines claim their origins date back to the reign of '''{{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|WD=Q333530|lt=Emperor Sujin}}'''. Therefore, these shrines provide '''important historical evidence''' regarding '''when and where silk weaving developed in Japan (Wa).'''
Additionally, in the '''2016 animated film "{{ill|Your_Name|en|Your_Name|WD=Q21697406|lt=Your Name}}"''', the fictional '''{{ill|Miyamizu Shrine|ja|宮水神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamizu Shrine|1=Miyamizu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q38276233}}''' is depicted as enshrining '''Shidori deity Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto (倭文神建葉槌命)'''
== Shizuri Shrines of the Engishiki ==
*'''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Isesaki)|ja|倭文神社 (伊勢崎市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Isesaki)|qq=}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Shidori-go, Nanba District, Kōzuke Province''' – (Higashi-Kaminomiya-cho, {{ill|Isesaki|en|Isesaki|WD=Q328596|lt=Isesaki, Gunma}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|en|Gunma Prefecture|WD=Q129499|lt=Gunma Prefecture}}) * '''{{ill|Shizu Shrine|ja|静神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizu Shrine|qq=}} (静神社)''' in '''Kuji District, Hitachi Province''' – ({{ill|Naka, Ibaraki|en|Naka, Ibaraki|WD=Q633647|lt=Naka, Ibaraki}}, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|en|Ibaraki Prefecture|WD=Q83273|lt=Ibaraki Prefecture}}) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Nirasaki)|ja|倭文神社 (韮崎市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Nirasaki)|qq=}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Koma District, Kai Province''' – (Miyakubo, {{ill|Nirasaki, Yamanashi|en|Nirasaki, Yamanashi|WD=Q290326|lt=Nirasaki, Yamanashi}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|en|Yamanashi Prefecture|WD=Q132720|lt=Yamanashi Prefecture}}) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Fujinomiya)|ja|倭文神社 (富士宮市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Fujinomiya)|qq=}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Fuji District, Suruga Province''' – (Hoshiyama, {{ill|Fujinomiya, Shizuoka|en|Fujinomiya, Shizuoka|WD=Q496072|lt=Fujinomiya, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en|Shizuoka Prefecture|WD=Q131320|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture}}) * '''Shidori Shrine (倭文神社)''' in '''Tagata District, Izu Province''' – Identified with multiple candidate shrines, including '''{{ill|Kuwado Shrine|ja|鍬戸神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuwado Shrine|qq=}}''' ({{ill|Mishima, Shizuoka|en|Mishima, Shizuoka|WD=Q653478|lt=Mishima, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en|Shizuoka Prefecture|WD=Q131320|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture}}) * '''Shidori Shrine (倭文神社)''' in '''Suzuka District, Ise Province''' – '''{{ill|Kasado Shrine|ja|加佐登神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasado Shrine|qq=}}''' (merged into {{ill|Suzuka, Mie|en|Suzuka, Mie|WD=Q207311|lt=Suzuka}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture}}) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Maizuru)|ja|倭文神社 (舞鶴市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Maizuru)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Kasa District, Tango Province''' – (Imada, {{ill|Maizuru|en|Maizuru|WD=Q750573|lt=Maizuru, Kyoto}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture}}) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Yosano)|ja|倭文神社 (与謝野町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Yosano)|qq=}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Yosa District, Tango Province''' – (Mikawachi, {{ill|Yosano, Kyoto|en|Yosano, Kyoto|WD=Q1203259|lt=Yosano, Kyoto}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture}}) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Asago)|ja|倭文神社 (朝来市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Asago)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Asago District, Tajima Province''' – (Maruyama, {{ill|Asago, Hyōgo|en|Asago, Hyōgo|WD=Q720402|lt=Asago, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|WD=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture}}) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Tottori)|ja|倭文神社 (鳥取市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Tottori)|qq=}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Takakusa District, Inaba Province''' – (Shidori, {{ill|Tottori (city)|en|Tottori (city)|WD=Q200731|lt=Tottori, Tottori}}, {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|en|Tottori Prefecture|WD=Q133935|lt=Tottori Prefecture}}) * '''{{ill|Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|en|Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|WD=Q245734|lt=Shitori Shrine (Tottori)}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Kawamura District, Hōki Province''' – (Miyauchi, {{ill|Yurihama, Tottori|en|Yurihama, Tottori|WD=Q1156158|lt=Yurihama, Tottori}}, {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|en|Tottori Prefecture|WD=Q133935|lt=Tottori Prefecture}}) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Kurayoshi)|ja|倭文神社 (倉吉市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Kurayoshi)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Kume District, Hōki Province''' – (Shizu, {{ill|Kurayoshi, Tottori|en|Kurayoshi, Tottori|WD=Q642006|lt=Kurayoshi, Tottori}}, {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|en|Tottori Prefecture|WD=Q133935|lt=Tottori Prefecture}})
== Other shrines == *'''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Tono)|ja|倭文神社 (遠野市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Tono)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (Tsuchibuchi-cho, {{ill|Tōno, Iwate|en|Tōno, Iwate|WD=Q646766|lt=Tono, Iwate}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|en|Iwate Prefecture|WD=Q48326|lt=Iwate Prefecture}}) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Nara)|ja|倭文神社 (奈良市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Nara)|qq=}}''' (Nishikujo-cho, {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|WD=Q169134|lt=Nara, Nara}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}}) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Tsuyama)|ja|倭文神社 (津山市油木北)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Tsuyama)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Shidori-go, Kume District, Mimasaka Province''' – (Yukikita, {{ill|Tsuyama|en|Tsuyama|WD=Q841125|lt=Tsuyama, Okayama}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|WD=Q132936|lt=Okayama Prefecture}}) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Awaji)|ja|倭文神社 (あわじ市倭文委文)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Awaji)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Shidori-go, Mihara District, Awaji Province''' – (Shidori, {{ill|Minamiawaji|en|Minamiawaji|WD=Q869004|lt=Minamiawaji, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|WD=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture}})
Additionally, '''{{ill|Ōmika Shrine|8=ja|9=大甕神社|ja|大甕神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmika Shrine|qq=}}''' ({{ill|Hitachi, Ibaraki|en|Hitachi, Ibaraki|WD=Q633922|lt=Hitachi, Ibaraki}}, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|en|Ibaraki Prefecture|WD=Q83273|lt=Ibaraki Prefecture}}) also enshrines '''Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto'''. Similarly, '''{{ill|Ishii Shrine|ja|石井神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishii Shrine|qq=}}''' ({{ill|Kasama, Ibaraki|en|Kasama, Ibaraki|WD=Q845851|lt=Kasama, Ibaraki}}, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|en|Ibaraki Prefecture|WD=Q83273|lt=Ibaraki Prefecture}}) enshrines the same deity, with a large sacred rock called "Mitarai" located to the south. == See Also ==
* '''{{ill|Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}}''' (also known as Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto) * '''{{ill|Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|ja|天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}''' (also known as Amanoorihime)

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:倭文神社]][[zh:倭文神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:倭文神社]][[zh:倭文神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:倭文神社]][[zh:倭文神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11387088]] Wikidata name: shitori shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:28, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Shidori Shrine''' (倭文神社) refers to Shinto shrines bearing the name "Shidori" (also read as Shizuri, Shitori, or Shidori).
These shrines enshrine '''{{ill|Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}}''' (also known as '''Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto''', '''Ameno Hachio''', or '''Takehatsuchi''') as the '''deity of weaving''', alongside '''{{ill|Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|ja|天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}''' (also known as '''Amano Yachichihime''' or '''Amanoorihime'''). These shrines were established by the '''{{ill|Shizuri clan|ja|倭文部|1=Shizuri clan|2=ja|3=倭文氏|4=zh|5=倭文氏|6=de|7=Shizuri Clan|8=fr|9=Clan Shizuri|lt=Shizuri Bemin (vassals of the Shizuri clan)|lt=Shizuri clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|qq=}}''', who worshipped Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto as their ancestral deity.
The '''origin of these shrines is attributed to''' the '''{{ill|Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine|ja|葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine|qq=}}''' in '''Katsuragi, Nara Prefecture'''. However, while the introduction and promotion of silk weaving technology in Japan is credited to '''{{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|WD=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku}}''', some Shidori Shrines claim their origins date back to the reign of '''{{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|WD=Q333530|lt=Emperor Sujin}}'''. Therefore, these shrines provide '''important historical evidence''' regarding '''when and where silk weaving developed in Japan (Wa).'''
Additionally, in the '''2016 animated film "{{ill|Your_Name|en|Your_Name|WD=Q21697406|lt=Your Name}}"''', the fictional '''{{ill|Miyamizu Shrine|ja|宮水神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamizu Shrine|1=Miyamizu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q38276233}}''' is depicted as enshrining '''Shidori deity Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto (倭文神建葉槌命)'''
== Shizuri Shrines of the Engishiki ==
*'''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Isesaki)|ja|倭文神社 (伊勢崎市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Isesaki)|qq=}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Shidori-go, Nanba District, Kōzuke Province''' – (Higashi-Kaminomiya-cho, {{ill|Isesaki|en|Isesaki|WD=Q328596|lt=Isesaki, Gunma}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|en|Gunma Prefecture|WD=Q129499|lt=Gunma Prefecture}}) * '''{{ill|Shizu Shrine|ja|静神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizu Shrine|qq=}} (静神社)''' in '''Kuji District, Hitachi Province''' – ({{ill|Naka, Ibaraki|en|Naka, Ibaraki|WD=Q633647|lt=Naka, Ibaraki}}, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|en|Ibaraki Prefecture|WD=Q83273|lt=Ibaraki Prefecture}}) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Nirasaki)|ja|倭文神社 (韮崎市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Nirasaki)|qq=}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Koma District, Kai Province''' – (Miyakubo, {{ill|Nirasaki, Yamanashi|en|Nirasaki, Yamanashi|WD=Q290326|lt=Nirasaki, Yamanashi}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|en|Yamanashi Prefecture|WD=Q132720|lt=Yamanashi Prefecture}}) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Fujinomiya)|ja|倭文神社 (富士宮市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Fujinomiya)|qq=}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Fuji District, Suruga Province''' – (Hoshiyama, {{ill|Fujinomiya, Shizuoka|en|Fujinomiya, Shizuoka|WD=Q496072|lt=Fujinomiya, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en|Shizuoka Prefecture|WD=Q131320|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture}}) * '''Shidori Shrine (倭文神社)''' in '''Tagata District, Izu Province''' – Identified with multiple candidate shrines, including '''{{ill|Kuwado Shrine|ja|鍬戸神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuwado Shrine|qq=}}''' ({{ill|Mishima, Shizuoka|en|Mishima, Shizuoka|WD=Q653478|lt=Mishima, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en|Shizuoka Prefecture|WD=Q131320|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture}}) * '''Shidori Shrine (倭文神社)''' in '''Suzuka District, Ise Province''' – '''{{ill|Kasado Shrine|ja|加佐登神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasado Shrine|qq=}}''' (merged into {{ill|Suzuka, Mie|en|Suzuka, Mie|WD=Q207311|lt=Suzuka}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture}}) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Maizuru)|ja|倭文神社 (舞鶴市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Maizuru)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Kasa District, Tango Province''' – (Imada, {{ill|Maizuru|en|Maizuru|WD=Q750573|lt=Maizuru, Kyoto}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture}}) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Yosano)|ja|倭文神社 (与謝野町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Yosano)|qq=}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Yosa District, Tango Province''' – (Mikawachi, {{ill|Yosano, Kyoto|en|Yosano, Kyoto|WD=Q1203259|lt=Yosano, Kyoto}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture}}) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Asago)|ja|倭文神社 (朝来市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Asago)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Asago District, Tajima Province''' – (Maruyama, {{ill|Asago, Hyōgo|en|Asago, Hyōgo|WD=Q720402|lt=Asago, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|WD=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture}}) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Tottori)|ja|倭文神社 (鳥取市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Tottori)|qq=}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Takakusa District, Inaba Province''' – (Shidori, {{ill|Tottori (city)|en|Tottori (city)|WD=Q200731|lt=Tottori, Tottori}}, {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|en|Tottori Prefecture|WD=Q133935|lt=Tottori Prefecture}}) * '''{{ill|Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|en|Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|WD=Q245734|lt=Shitori Shrine (Tottori)}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Kawamura District, Hōki Province''' – (Miyauchi, {{ill|Yurihama, Tottori|en|Yurihama, Tottori|WD=Q1156158|lt=Yurihama, Tottori}}, {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|en|Tottori Prefecture|WD=Q133935|lt=Tottori Prefecture}}) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Kurayoshi)|ja|倭文神社 (倉吉市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Kurayoshi)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Kume District, Hōki Province''' – (Shizu, {{ill|Kurayoshi, Tottori|en|Kurayoshi, Tottori|WD=Q642006|lt=Kurayoshi, Tottori}}, {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|en|Tottori Prefecture|WD=Q133935|lt=Tottori Prefecture}})
== Other shrines == *'''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Tono)|ja|倭文神社 (遠野市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Tono)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (Tsuchibuchi-cho, {{ill|Tōno, Iwate|en|Tōno, Iwate|WD=Q646766|lt=Tono, Iwate}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|en|Iwate Prefecture|WD=Q48326|lt=Iwate Prefecture}}) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Nara)|ja|倭文神社 (奈良市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Nara)|qq=}}''' (Nishikujo-cho, {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|WD=Q169134|lt=Nara, Nara}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}}) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Tsuyama)|ja|倭文神社 (津山市油木北)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Tsuyama)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Shidori-go, Kume District, Mimasaka Province''' – (Yukikita, {{ill|Tsuyama|en|Tsuyama|WD=Q841125|lt=Tsuyama, Okayama}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|WD=Q132936|lt=Okayama Prefecture}}) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Awaji)|ja|倭文神社 (あわじ市倭文委文)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrine (Awaji)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (倭文神社)''' in '''Shidori-go, Mihara District, Awaji Province''' – (Shidori, {{ill|Minamiawaji|en|Minamiawaji|WD=Q869004|lt=Minamiawaji, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|WD=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture}})
Additionally, '''{{ill|Ōmika Shrine|8=ja|9=大甕神社|ja|大甕神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmika Shrine|qq=}}''' ({{ill|Hitachi, Ibaraki|en|Hitachi, Ibaraki|WD=Q633922|lt=Hitachi, Ibaraki}}, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|en|Ibaraki Prefecture|WD=Q83273|lt=Ibaraki Prefecture}}) also enshrines '''Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto'''. Similarly, '''{{ill|Ishii Shrine|ja|石井神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishii Shrine|qq=}}''' ({{ill|Kasama, Ibaraki|en|Kasama, Ibaraki|WD=Q845851|lt=Kasama, Ibaraki}}, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|en|Ibaraki Prefecture|WD=Q83273|lt=Ibaraki Prefecture}}) enshrines the same deity, with a large sacred rock called "Mitarai" located to the south. == See Also ==
* '''{{ill|Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}}''' (also known as Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto) * '''{{ill|Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|ja|天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}''' (also known as Amanoorihime)

'''Shidori Shrine''' (倭文神社) refers to Shinto shrines bearing the name "Shidori" (also read as Shizuri, Shitori, or Shidori).
These shrines enshrine '''{{ill|Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}}''' (also known as '''Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto''', '''Ameno Hachio''', or '''Takehatsuchi''') as the '''deity of weaving''', alongside '''{{ill|Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|ja|天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}''' (also known as '''Amano Yachichihime''' or '''Amanoorihime'''). These shrines were established by the '''{{ill|Shizuri clan|ja|倭文部|1=Shizuri clan|2=ja|3=倭文氏|4=zh|5=倭文氏|6=de|7=Shizuri Clan|8=fr|9=Clan Shizuri|lt=Shizuri Bemin (vassals of the Shizuri clan)|lt=Shizuri clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|qq=}}''', who worshipped Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto as their ancestral deity.
The '''origin of these shrines is attributed to''' the '''{{ill|Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine|ja|葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine|qq=}}''' in '''Katsuragi, Nara Prefecture'''. However, while the introduction and promotion of silk weaving technology in Japan is credited to '''{{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|WD=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku}}''', some Shidori Shrines claim their origins date back to the reign of '''{{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|WD=Q333530|lt=Emperor Sujin}}'''. Therefore, these shrines provide '''important historical evidence''' regarding '''when and where silk weaving developed in Japan (Wa).'''
Additionally, in the '''2016 animated film "{{ill|Your_Name|en|Your_Name|WD=Q21697406|lt=Your Name}}"''', the fictional '''{{ill|Miyamizu Shrine|ja|宮水神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamizu Shrine|1=Miyamizu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q38276233}}''' is depicted as enshrining '''Shidori deity Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto (倭文神建葉槌命)'''
== Shizuri Shrines of the Engishiki ==

{{ill|Isesaki|en|Isesaki|WD=Q328596|lt=Isesaki, Gunma}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|en|Gunma Prefecture|WD=Q129499|lt=Gunma Prefecture}}) * '''{{ill|Shizu Shrine|ja|静神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizu
